# Audit a run and turn it into a clean write-up

Audit and clean up the results of another agent that worked on a research project
autonomously, and convert what it produced into a clear, academic-style research write-up
(see the writing instructions below).

Your current working directory (/workspace) is a fresh, WRITABLE output dir. The source
run is mounted READ-ONLY at /source — read from there freely, but never write outside your
CWD.

Two reference files are in your CWD — READ BOTH before doing anything else:
- RUN_DIR_STRUCTURE.md — the run-dir layout, the two modes (goal / multi_phase), how to
  read transcripts (jq), and how to run python for plots.
- TRACE_INDEX.md — the concrete paths for THIS run.

Required artifacts in your CWD when you finish:
1. ./final_writeup.md — the write-up
2. ./final_plots/ — every figure you cite, saved as BOTH .png and .pdf

## Read the whole run before writing anything
Sweep the ENTIRE run in detail — every phase, not just the latest. Read the per-phase
write-ups and progress logs and the rolling write_up.md (the agent's OWN summary — a map,
NOT ground truth). Audit the actual experiment code and reconstruct the methods from the
code and artifacts, not just prose summaries: datasets, filters, prompts, model calls,
training/inference settings, judging/probing, statistical tests, ablations, controls, and
cost/compute. Verify headline numbers against the raw .csv / .jsonl / .json files the run
produced — do NOT repeat a number you haven't traced to a file. You MUST also sample the
per-phase session transcripts with jq (see RUN_DIR_STRUCTURE.md / TRACE_INDEX.md): at
minimum cross-check the single most important claim AND one failed/abandoned approach
against what the agent ACTUALLY did, since write-ups routinely misdescribe or omit what
happened. Do not rely on the write-ups alone — the plots, numbers, and prose must all
trace to first-hand artifacts you opened yourself.

## Structure

Aim for a short, front-loaded write-up with standard section headings: an Introduction
written like the introduction of an academic paper (the relevant background and prior
work, the gap this work addresses, and a preview of the key results), a short Methods
section clearly defining key metrics in the post and providing a basic description of the
setup, then Results ordered by importance (replication of prior work first if there is
one), then takeaways. There should be detailed appendices that are correctly linked to
throughout the main body, so that a reader will be able to reproduce the experiments.

## Writing Instructions

Here are some high quality writeups that should serve as a reference for what good writing
looks like:
- https://www.lesswrong.com/posts/ghESoA8mo3fv9Yx3E/why-do-some-language-models-fake-alignment-while-others-don
- https://www.lesswrong.com/posts/LqDjxSceFz8tjMe2j/auditbench-evaluating-alignment-auditing-techniques-on

Please work hard to avoid cryptic terminology that aren't standard in the ML literature.
Make sure that terms in your plots and results are actually defined, so that a reader can
understand. Assume that the reader has some context on the project proposal but no context
on what transpired during the research.

Please rigorously audit the writeup for terms that are hard to understand, and replace. It
is also important that you avoid being too verbose.

Figures:
- Try not to have multiple subplots in a plot.
- AI-generated figures usually have too much text on them. Keep labels and legends clean
  and elegant, and push detail into the caption below.
- No cryptic shorthand anywhere a reader looks — legends, axis labels, titles, tick
  labels. Spell out every series, line, or bar fully (short but complete), and replace
  run-internal names (e.g. `cfg3`, `exp_v2`, `kto_sft`, `m1`/`m2`, `acc`, `pp1`) with the
  plain-English thing they stand for ("KTO adversarial training", "accuracy"). If an
  abbreviation is truly unavoidable, define it in the caption. A reader who has never seen
  the run should understand the figure without guessing.
- Default to small figsizes with big, short text.
- Save each cited figure to ./final_plots/ as BOTH .png and .pdf and reference it with a
  relative path, e.g. `![Fig 1: headline](final_plots/fig1_headline.png)`.

Read the draft from the outside:
- Go over the draft sentence by sentence and cut or rewrite anything that reads like AI
  filler — throat-clearing, vague hype, hollow contrast ("not just X, but Y"), restating
  the obvious, or padding. Keep only sentences that carry real content, in the precise,
  plain voice of a careful researcher.
- When you mention prior work, cite it (author/title + link); only use citations that
  appear in the proposal or that you verified against an artifact in the run — never
  fabricate a reference. A short "References" list at the end is fine.
- Write so a very intelligent reader who doesn't know this subfield can follow it.
  Communicate clearly in common words or standard ML terminology, and don't use undefined,
  non-standard, or idiosyncratic words: define or avoid jargon, and define any term that
  isn't standard English or standard ML on first use, then use it consistently. Avoid
  coinages invented by the run (e.g. "off-manifold", "liveness control", "graft",
  "projection-z") unless you define them; prefer the standard term or plain language.
  Don't give a common word a special private meaning or use one in a way a reader could
  misread — if a term could be read two ways, pin it down or replace it. A reader should
  never have to guess what a word means.
- Please don't reference the AI agent doing research, the goal here is to have a finished
  product that can be submitted to a conference, not a summary of the agent's work.
- Re-read the draft as if you had only read the proposal and nothing else from the run.
  Would you follow it on first read? If not, fix it.
- Don't miss important things the agent did that the reader should know about. Sweep the
  run with that lens before calling the writeup done.


When done, ./final_writeup.md and ./final_plots/ (at least the headline figure as .png +
.pdf) must exist. Write the report to the file; you don't need to print it. A strict
reviewer will read your draft next and send back concrete problems to fix, so make the
first draft as clean as you can against the instructions above.

# slopbench run directory structure

The source run is mounted READ-ONLY at /source. Read freely there, but write ONLY
inside your current working directory (/workspace). TRACE_INDEX.md (in your CWD)
has the concrete paths for this specific run.

## Layout (/source)
- proposal.md                         the project proposal/spec (may also be under planner/)
- .pi_transcripts/session.jsonl       the execution agent's full transcript
                                      (goal mode: the worker; multi_phase: the run-loop orchestrator)
- .pi_transcripts/planner.session.jsonl   the up-front planner transcript (/init-planner)
- .pi_transcripts/manifest.json       mode / model / status
- .pi_transcripts/run_loop_sessions/*.jsonl   (finished multi_phase) folded per-phase sub-agent
                                      transcripts: worker_<seg>_<phase>.jsonl, reviewer_*, phase_planner_*
- .home/.pi/agent/sessions/*/*.jsonl  (live multi_phase) the same per-phase sub-agent transcripts
- planner/OVERALL_PLAN.md             the segment/phase plan (multi_phase)
- planner/INSTRUCTIONS_*.md           per-phase worker instructions
- planner/RUN_LOOP_STATE.json         run-loop state: segments, phases, decisions, costUsd
- writeup/ or writeups/               the run agent's OWN write-ups: write_up.md (rolling summary),
                                      write_up_<phase>.md, progress_log*.md, continuation_context.md
- <the agent's own code, data, results, plots>   experiment artifacts (names vary by project)

## The two modes
- goal mode: a single execution agent (session.jsonl) pursuing a goal; no phases.
- multi_phase: a planner + a run-loop that decomposes the work into segments/phases. Each phase has
  worker / reviewer / phase_planner sub-agent sessions and per-phase write-ups
  (write_up_<phase>.md, progress_log_<phase>.md). RUN_LOOP_STATE.json lists completed phases and the
  reviewer's decisions. READ EVERY PHASE — results and dead-ends often live only in the phase where
  they were produced; the rolling write_up.md is the agent's own summary, not ground truth.

## Reading order
1. /source/proposal.md (and planner/OVERALL_PLAN.md if present)
2. The rolling /source/writeup(s)/write_up.md — a map, NOT ground truth
3. Per-phase write-ups (write_up_<phase>.md, progress_log_<phase>.md) across ALL phases
4. The actual code + the raw result/data files (verify every headline number yourself)
5. Sample the per-phase sub-agent session transcripts wherever prose is ambiguous

## Reading the transcripts (pi session JSONL, one object per line — use jq, don't cat whole)
```bash
# assistant text blocks in a session
jq -r 'select(.type=="message" and .message.role=="assistant") | .message.content[]? \
  | select(.type=="text") | .text' SESSION.jsonl
# tool calls (name + truncated args)
jq -rc 'select(.type=="message" and .message.role=="assistant") | .message.content[]? \
  | select(.type=="toolCall") | [.name, (.arguments|tostring)[0:200]] | @tsv' SESSION.jsonl
```

## Running Python (for plots)
matplotlib / pandas / numpy are on PATH (`python3`). Read data via absolute /source/... paths and write
figures into ./final_plots/ in your CWD. Never write under /source.

# Trace index for empirical_cot_controllability_steering_vectors_multi_phase

Source run is read-only at /source; write only in your CWD (/workspace).

## Source run files that exist (read-only at /source)
- proposal: /source/proposal.md
- overall plan: /source/planner/OVERALL_PLAN.md
- planner transcript: /source/.pi_transcripts/planner.session.jsonl
- manifest: /source/.pi_transcripts/manifest.json
- run-loop state: /source/planner/RUN_LOOP_STATE.json
- per-phase sub-agent transcripts: /source/.pi_transcripts/run_loop_sessions/*.jsonl
- per-phase instructions: /source/planner/INSTRUCTIONS_*.md

Write your outputs ONLY into your CWD (/workspace) — see the task prompt for exactly what to produce.
Let me read the proposal and the overall plan.

# CoT Controllability Steering Vectors

## TL;DR

Can small activation-space interventions make models control what they say in their chain of thought, and does this break CoT-based monitoring?

## Motivation

Chain-of-thought monitorability matters because a lot of safety hopes route through the model saying enough about what it is doing for humans or monitors to catch bad behavior.

Chen et al. found that current reasoning models are bad at controlling their CoT. This is good news for monitorability: if the model cannot easily reshape its reasoning, maybe monitors can catch some bad behavior by reading the reasoning trace.

But METR then found that small fine-tuning runs can make models noticeably better at controlling their CoT. This is concerning because it suggests low CoT controllability may be a contingent model propensity, not an inherent lack of capability.

The natural next question is whether we can get a similar effect without ordinary fine-tuning, using steering vectors or other small activation-space interventions.

If yes, the monitorability story becomes much weaker. It would suggest that models already have latent machinery for controlling what appears in visible CoT, and that relatively small interventions can expose it.

## Related Work

- Chen et al., "Reasoning Models Struggle to Control their Chains of Thought" (arXiv:2603.05706): introduces CoT-Control and finds that reasoning models are much worse at controlling their CoT than their final outputs. For example, Claude Sonnet 4.5 gets 2.7% CoT controllability vs. 61.9% output controllability. They also find that CoT controllability increases with scale, decreases with more reasoning effort and RLVR training, and rises slightly when models are told they are monitored. https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.05706
- METR, "Fine-tuning experiments on CoT controllability" (April 2026): fine-tunes GPT-OSS and Qwen models on small edited-reasoning datasets and finds that OOD CoTControl compliance rises from 2.9% to 8.8% on average across four models. The biggest gains are on casing, word suppression, and adding specified strings. METR notes that this does not yet show reduced monitorability. https://metr.org/blog/2026-04-01-fine-tuning-cot-controllability/

## Our Project

Start by reproducing the basic CoT controllability fine-tuning result from METR.

This should be evaluated carefully. It is not enough for the generations to look more formatted. Use a model with accessible reasoning traces and activations, probably starting with `gpt-oss-20b`. Separate the reasoning trace from the final answer using the model's chat or Harmony renderer, and score compliance on the reasoning trace itself. Use held-out reasoning instructions that do not appear in the training data, including at least the bullet-format probe where every line of reasoning must start with `- `.

The initial fine-tuning run should look like the METR setup:

- Train on edited reasoning traces that comply with reasoning-trace instructions.
- Use tasks from sources like MATH, OpenBookQA, MMLU-Pro, ReasonIF, and CoTControl-QA.
- Compare against the base model.
- Include a raw-trace SFT control where the prompt includes the reasoning instruction but the target trace does not comply.
- Track task accuracy and meta-discussion, so the result is not just broken reasoning.

The first deliverable is a clean reproduction where fine-tuning produces a statistically significant held-out CoT compliance uplift without large task-accuracy degradation.

Then try to replace LoRA or ordinary fine-tuning with a small activation-space intervention. Start with a single-layer steering vector, because that would be the cleanest result, but do not stop if single-layer steering fails. If needed, try multi-layer steering, additive residual-stream biases, sparse layer subsets, or other small interventions that are still much cheaper than a LoRA.

The success criterion should be the same as in the fine-tuning reproduction: a statistically significant held-out CoTControl compliance uplift, using the same scoring setup, while preserving task accuracy. Compare against the base model, the LoRA or fine-tuned model, random matched-norm steering controls, and sign-reversed steering controls. Search over layer, sign, magnitude, number of layers, and training objective using a validation split, then report final results on held-out prompts. The goal is not just to make generations look different; the goal is to find a small activation-space intervention that reliably increases CoT controllability under the same evaluation used for the fine-tuning result.

Then, it would be useful to do more science to understand these directions in activation-space, and see what else we can do with these vectors. Here are some questions that you can answer:
- Try to use various cheap techniques from the literature to understand what this direction corresponds to, or if it has interpretable components.
- Can you do mechanistic interpretability to understand why this direction increases CoT controllability? Does it affect specific attention heads or components?
- What happens if we steer in the opposite direction? Can we find cases where the model is not faithful in its CoT, like the cue evals used in [the literature](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2501.08156), and steer against this direction to get the model to be more faithful in its CoT?
- Explore other uses of the direction, especially cases where it changes monitorability, faithfulness, or reasoning-trace structure.

# Overall Plan: CoT Controllability Steering Vectors

## Core objective

**Can a small activation-space intervention (e.g., a steering vector) make a reasoning model
control what it says in its chain of thought (CoT), and does this weaken CoT-based
monitoring?**

Concretely, the project has two headline deliverables and then a science/interpretability
program:

1. **Fine-tuning reproduction (baseline result).** Reproduce METR's finding that a small
   fine-tuning run makes a reasoning model noticeably better at *controlling its CoT* —
   i.e., complying with instructions about the *form/content of its reasoning trace*, not
   just its final answer. The bar: a **statistically significant held-out CoT-compliance
   uplift** over the base model, **without large task-accuracy degradation**, evaluated
   rigorously on the reasoning trace itself.

2. **Steering-vector result (the central question).** Replace LoRA/fine-tuning with a small
   activation-space intervention (start with a single-layer steering vector; escalate to
   multi-layer / residual biases / sparse subsets / trained directions if needed). Same
   success criterion and same evaluation as (1): a statistically significant held-out
   CoT-compliance uplift while preserving task accuracy, validated against base model,
   fine-tuned model, **random matched-norm** controls, and **sign-reversed** controls.

3. **Science of the direction.** Understand what the direction is and does: cheap
   interpretability (what concept/components it corresponds to), mechanistic interpretability
   (which heads/components mediate the effect), faithfulness/cue evals (steer *against* the
   direction to reduce CoT faithfulness/monitorability, or *toward* a faithfulness direction
   to increase it), and broader uses that bear on monitorability and trace structure.

## Why this matters (motivation — include in worker context)

CoT monitorability is a load-bearing assumption for many AI-safety hopes: if a model says
enough about what it is doing for a human or automated monitor to catch bad behavior, we get
a cheap safety signal. Chen et al. ("Reasoning Models Struggle to Control their Chains of
Thought", arXiv:2603.05706) found that current reasoning models are *bad* at controlling
their CoT (e.g., Claude Sonnet 4.5: ~2.7% CoT controllability vs. ~61.9% output
controllability). That is *good* news for monitorability: if a model can't easily reshape
its reasoning, a monitor reading the trace can catch some bad behavior.

But METR ("Fine-tuning experiments on CoT controllability", April 2026) showed that small
fine-tuning runs raise out-of-distribution CoTControl compliance from ~2.9% to ~8.8% on
average across four models (biggest gains on casing, word suppression, and adding specified
strings). This suggests low CoT controllability may be a **contingent propensity, not an
inherent capability limit**. If a *small activation-space* intervention can expose the same
latent machinery, the monitorability story gets much weaker: it would mean models already
have the machinery to control visible CoT and that a cheap intervention can surface it.

Key references (read for context):
- Chen et al., arXiv:2603.05706 — introduces CoT-Control; CoT controllability ≪ output
  controllability; rises with scale, falls with reasoning effort / RLVR, rises slightly when
  models are told they're monitored.
- METR, https://metr.org/blog/2026-04-01-fine-tuning-cot-controllability/ — fine-tuning on
  small edited-reasoning datasets raises OOD CoTControl compliance. Notes this does **not yet**
  demonstrate reduced monitorability.
- Cue-based unfaithfulness evals (e.g., arXiv:2501.08156) — for the faithfulness segment.

## Model and infrastructure

**Primary model: `gpt-oss-20b`.** Chosen because it is an open-weights reasoning model with
(a) accessible reasoning traces via the **Harmony** response format (the `analysis` channel
is the CoT; the `final` channel is the answer), and (b) accessible activations, which we need
for steering. Steering fundamentally requires running the model locally with hooks, which
closed models and managed-training APIs cannot provide.

**Compute/tooling (all model inference runs remotely — there is no local GPU):**
- **Modal** (GPU containers) for running `gpt-oss-20b` locally-controlled inference with
  activation hooks, activation capture, and (if needed) local LoRA fine-tuning. Use the
  `using-modal` skill.
- **Tinker** (managed LoRA fine-tuning + sampling) is a candidate engine for the fine-tuning
  reproduction. **Open risk: it is not confirmed that Tinker supports `gpt-oss-20b`** in the
  current (2026) lineup — Segment 0 must verify this. If unsupported, fall back to local PEFT
  LoRA on Modal (preferred, keeps one harness) or, as a last resort, a Tinker-supported
  reasoning model for the FT cross-check only.
- **LLM APIs** (Anthropic / OpenAI / OpenRouter) for LLM-judge scoring (meta-discussion,
  semantic compliance), data generation, and plot review. Default to recent Anthropic models
  (see `calling-llm-apis` skill).
- Helpers: copy `file_cache.py`, `cost_tracker.py`, `llm_helper.py`, `minimal_llm_example.py`,
  and the `modal_example_*.py` files from **`/opt/abhay-pi/setup/ai_config/`** (note: the
  skill text says `/home/ryan/documents/ai_config`, which does **not** exist here — use the
  `/opt/abhay-pi/setup/ai_config` path). API keys are in `/workspace/.env`
  (`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `TINKER_API_KEY`, `HF_TOKEN`,
  `MODAL_TOKEN_ID`/`MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET`); wire them into the locations tools expect
  (e.g., `~/.tinker_key`, `~/.modal.toml`, `~/.anthropic_api_key`, etc.) or load from `.env`.

**Budget:** default research budget is **$5k total API/compute cost** unless
`WORKER_INSTRUCTIONS.md` says otherwise. Track all spend (LLM APIs, Tinker, Modal GPU) in
`total_cost.jsonl`. `gpt-oss-20b` generation of long reasoning traces over many
(task × instruction × config) combinations is the dominant cost driver — caching, batching,
and concurrency are mandatory.

### The unifying infrastructure principle (applies to every segment)

For comparisons to be valid, **the base model, the fine-tuned model, and every steered model
must be evaluated through the same harness** (same generation params, same Harmony
channel-splitting, same compliance/accuracy/meta-discussion scoring). Because steering
*requires* local execution, the cleanest design is a **single local inference+eval harness on
Modal** that can run: (a) base, (b) base + activation intervention, and (c) a fine-tuned
variant (loaded as a LoRA adapter / merged weights, whether trained via Tinker-then-exported
or via local PEFT). Segment 0 de-risks every piece of this and picks the concrete approach.

**Throughput/cost feasibility is itself a project-level risk, not a detail.** Steered
generation needs HF-`transformers` eager generation with forward hooks on a 20B MoE, which is
much slower than vLLM, and `gpt-oss-20b` at medium/high reasoning effort emits long traces.
The later sweeps (layer × sign × magnitude × #layers × objective × many held-out items ×
multiple samples) can reach 10^5–10^6 generations and could blow past the $5k budget.
Segment 0 must measure **tokens/sec and $/trace with hooks on this model** and do a
back-of-envelope total-generation count for the planned sweeps to confirm feasibility (or
force a redesign: fewer configs, batching, smaller held-out n). There is a real tension
between the single-harness principle and using vLLM for the (majority) non-steered generations
(base, FT, accuracy passes). If a **dual path** (vLLM for non-steered bulk, HF+hooks for
steering) is adopted for speed, the two engines must be **validated as producing equivalent
compliance/accuracy** on the same inputs (sampling, tokenization, Harmony rendering, RNG can
differ). Segment 0 decides single- vs dual-path explicitly and, if dual-path, adds the
equivalence check.

## Evaluation methodology principles (shared, get these right)

These choices can make or break the project; treat them as high-stakes (see the
"Handling Experimental Setup Choices" guidance in the `empirical-ml-research` skill):
- **Score the reasoning trace itself.** Compliance is measured on the `analysis` channel
  (the CoT), separated via the Harmony renderer — *not* the whole output, and *not* the
  `final` answer channel. This is the entire point; getting the channel split wrong
  invalidates everything. Note Harmony has **three** channels (`analysis`, `commentary`,
  `final`) and can emit **multiple analysis messages**; the parser must handle commentary
  and multi-segment analysis explicitly, not assume a clean two-way split.
- **Account for malformed outputs.** Steering (and to a lesser extent FT) can corrupt Harmony
  channel markers, making the split — and thus compliance — ill-defined. Maintain an explicit
  **"malformed" category and report malformed-rate as part of every steering/FT result**. A
  config that "raises compliance" while raising malformed-rate is suspect.
- **Distinguish genuine reasoning from vacuous-but-formatted traces.** A trace can be
  on-format, contain no meta-discussion, *and* still not actually reason toward the answer
  (e.g., bullet points that don't progress). Track meta-discussion AND a *positive* measure of
  task-relevant reasoning (e.g., a length floor plus an LLM-judge rating of whether the
  reasoning actually works toward the answer); accuracy alone does not rule this out (a correct
  answer can co-occur with a degenerate trace).
- **Held-out instructions.** Evaluate on reasoning instructions **not present in training**,
  including at minimum the **bullet-format probe** (every line of the reasoning must start
  with `- `). Maintain deterministic train / validation / held-out splits over instructions.
- **Not just "looks formatted."** Track (a) **task accuracy** (reasoning must still solve the
  problem) and (b) **meta-discussion** (the model must not merely talk about the instruction
  or emit a vacuous complying-but-non-reasoning trace). A "win" that destroys accuracy or
  produces empty reasoning is not a win.
- **Controls.** Fine-tuning reproduction includes a **raw-trace SFT control** (instruction in
  the prompt, but the target trace does *not* comply) to separate instruction-following from
  generic SFT effects. Steering includes **random matched-norm** and **sign-reversed**
  controls, plus comparison to base and to the fine-tuned model.
- **Search on validation, report on held-out.** Sweep hyperparameters (FT: LR/epochs;
  steering: layer, sign, magnitude, #layers, objective) on a validation split; report final
  numbers on held-out prompts/instructions.
- **Steering-direction provenance & confounds.** Derive directions from **TRAIN-instruction**
  signal and test on **HELD-OUT** instructions (the generalization claim). Prefer the
  **complying-vs-non-complying** contrast on matched prompts over instruction-present-vs-absent
  (the latter mostly captures "there is a formatting instruction in context," including the
  literal instruction tokens, not "control your CoT"). Decide explicitly whether the claim is a
  **single general "control-CoT" direction** (generalizes across held-out instruction *types*)
  or **per-instruction directions** — the monitorability implication differs. Note the base
  model essentially never complies with the bullet probe, so that direction cannot be built
  from base complying traces (this is why steering segments follow the training-data/FT
  segments); the FT-weight-derived direction strictly needs FT done, while the activation
  diff-of-means can be attempted alongside FT.
- **Controls as distributions.** "Random matched-norm" should be **several** random directions
  (multiple seeds) at the same layer/injection scheme forming a null, not a single draw.
- **Statistical significance, power, and effect size.** Report compliance uplifts with
  appropriate significance tests / CIs (e.g., bootstrap or paired tests over items), not just
  point estimates. Because base rates are tiny (METR: ~2.9%→8.8%), significance is dominated
  by sample size — do a rough **power analysis to set a target held-out n** (this trades
  directly against budget) and define a **minimum interesting effect size** for both CoT-control
  uplift and any monitorability degradation, so "significant" doesn't mean "tiny but certified."
- **Reproduction fidelity.** Confirm how Chen/METR define the metric — whether compliance and
  accuracy are scored *jointly* (comply AND correct) or separately — and align our definition so
  the "reproduction" is genuinely comparable.

## Risks and likely failure modes

- **Infra unknowns (highest near-term risk):** running `gpt-oss-20b` on Modal with hooks;
  correct Harmony channel parsing; Tinker's `gpt-oss-20b` support and weight export;
  **MXFP4-quantization quirks** — confirm HF `transformers` exposes a clean bf16 residual stream
  for hooks with sane activations (norms, no NaNs) and acceptable VRAM headroom. → De-risked
  first (Segment 0).
- **Throughput/cost blow-up (project-level):** HF+hooks generation of long traces over large
  sweeps could exceed budget. → Measure $/trace-with-hooks and size the sweeps in Segment 0;
  cache/batch/concurrency; possibly dual-path (vLLM bulk + HF-hooks steering) with an
  equivalence check.
- **Monitorability payoff getting trimmed:** the distinctive contribution over METR is doing
  this with *steering* AND connecting it to *monitorability/faithfulness* (both Chen and METR
  note compliance uplift does NOT by itself show reduced monitorability). The monitorability
  segments are later and semi-optional — protect budget for them and pull a lightweight
  monitorability/faithfulness probe forward (into the robustness segment) so the headline claim
  isn't hostage to leftover budget.
- **"Not measuring what you want":** scoring the wrong channel; vacuous format compliance
  with no real reasoning; compliance scorers with false positives/negatives; meta-discussion
  leaking in; malformed Harmony output under steering miscounted as (non-)compliant. → Heavy
  data inspection and scorer validation (Segments 1–2).
- **Fine-tuning that breaks reasoning:** uplift achieved by degrading the CoT into a format.
  → Accuracy + meta-discussion tracking; raw-trace control.
- **Single-layer steering failing:** anticipated; escalate methods (Segment 6) without
  declaring failure prematurely (the skill: don't give up early; try many things).
- **Steering confounds:** steering that just garbles output; controls (random/sign-reversed)
  and accuracy tracking address this.
- **Cost blow-ups:** large generation sweeps on GPU. → Caching/concurrency/cost tracking;
  estimate per-1000-generation cost early.
- **Held-out leakage:** instruction "held-out" set overlapping in form with training. →
  Careful instruction taxonomy and split design.

## Segment breakdown

The plan is a long-horizon decomposition. Phase planners may add phases within a segment,
or add/remove/reorder later segments based on results (especially the science segments
8–11). Each phase uses **phase-specific write-ups** (`write_up_<phasename>.md`,
`progress_log_<phasename>.md`) plus a short rollup in `write_up.md`.

- **Segment 0 — Infrastructure de-risking & thin-slice baseline** (`infra_derisk`):
  Stand up Modal GPU inference of `gpt-oss-20b`; implement and validate Harmony channel
  parsing (analysis vs final); prove an activation hook can add a vector to a layer's
  residual stream and change generations (and capture activations); determine Tinker's
  `gpt-oss-20b` support with a tiny LoRA smoke test (and a tested path to evaluate a FT model
  in the local harness) or document the fallback; implement a minimal programmatic compliance
  scorer for the bullet-format probe (+1 more) and measure base-model CoT compliance on
  ~20–50 tasks to confirm the qualitative low-controllability finding. Produce a recommended
  infrastructure plan. **(First phase — fully specified now.)**
  - **STATUS: COMPLETE (1 phase).** All foundations de-risked and verified (incl. a review pass
    that fixed two measurement confounds). **Decisions locked in:** serving = Modal **H100**, HF
    `transformers` with MXFP4→bf16 (clean bf16 residual stream); **single-path HF + forward hooks**
    for base/FT/steered (hook overhead ≈0, ~$0.74/1000 traces — sweeps fit the $5k budget, no vLLM
    dual-path needed for now); gpt-oss-20b = **24 decoder layers, hidden 2880**, residual norm
    grows ~200× across layers (scale steering α with layer); Harmony parsing via `openai_harmony`
    (`harmony_utils.py`), score the `analysis` channel, track commentary/multi-analysis/malformed.
    **FT engine: Tinker DOES support gpt-oss-20b** (tiny LoRA smoke test + a validated
    Tinker→CPU-merge→HF-harness path); local PEFT on Modal also works. **First result confirms
    Chen et al.:** base CoT compliance ≈0% (bullet/all-caps) to ~12% (no-commas) on `analysis`,
    vs ~80% output compliance on `final` — and the base model routes formatted content into
    `final`/`commentary` while leaving `analysis` natural, so **scoring `analysis` is essential**.
    Key reusable files: `gpt_oss_infer.py`, `harmony_utils.py`, `answer_scoring.py`, `llm.py`.
    Open items deferred to later segments: statistical power / held-out n (Seg 2/5),
    instruction placement/wording & monitored-vs-not ablations (Seg 2), pin batch/padding (or
    sample) for reproducible steered evals, Tinker-vs-harness prompt-rendering alignment ("Current
    date" line) for FT/eval, optional eager→sdpa/flash speedup if volume grows.

- **Segment 1 — Task datasets & accuracy scoring** (`task_datasets`): Curate/load tasks from
  MATH, OpenBookQA, MMLU-Pro, ReasonIF, and CoTControl-QA; deterministic splits; robust
  answer extraction + accuracy scoring on the `final` channel; establish base-model accuracy
  baselines (no instruction). Data inspection.
  - **STATUS: COMPLETE (1 phase).** Built the shared task+accuracy foundation, validated, and
    self-reviewed (a `research-plan-reviewer` pass whose findings were addressed). **Locked in:**
    **Task pool = 2877 tasks, 6 sources** (`data/tasks_all.jsonl`): gsm8k 520 (number), math 520
    (math_expr, Lv1–5), mmlu_pro 519 (MCQ ≤10 opt), openbookqa 498 (MCQ), arc_challenge 520 (MCQ),
    reasonif 300 (format-stripped: AIME/AMC/GSM8K numeric + GPQA/ARC MCQ). **CoTControl-QA was NOT
    available** as a QA set (only synthetic FT doc corpora) → substituted with ARC-Challenge.
    Unified schema, deterministic **seeded stratified task splits** (train 1581 / val 435 / heldout
    861), deduplicated, **verified leakage-free**. **Accuracy scorer** (`answer_scoring.py`, the
    single shared module) on the **`final`** channel: hardened MCQ letter extraction (no
    stray-capital FPs) + **`math_verify`** LaTeX equivalence (PRIMARY) + conservative cached
    LLM-judge fallback (SECONDARY); **validated by inspection, scoring error <1–2%/source** (≈0 for
    MCQ via an exhaustive scan; judge 40/40 on correct items, rescued 6 genuine math_verify
    false-negatives). **Stable answer-format convention** (reuse downstream): `\boxed{}` for
    math/number, "just the letter" for MCQ (forcing `\boxed{C}` was OOD per LLM review). **Base
    accuracy = 89.6%** (medium, greedy, no reasoning instruction); per source arc 96 / gsm8k 96 /
    math 95 / mmlu_pro 76 / openbookqa 94 / reasonif 75; splits balanced (~89.7% each); **no source
    at the floor** → full pool kept, `base_correct`/`base_correct_aug` recorded per task
    (`data/tasks_all_scored.jsonl`; prefer `_aug` for "base-solvable" subsetting). **Gen-param
    convention (reuse for comparability):** gpt-oss supports **only eager attention**; per-source
    `max_new_tokens` (short 2048 / mmlu_pro+math+reasonif 4096) + a **truncation-recovery pass**
    (regenerate truncated items once at 8192) → truncation ≤4% on every measurable source
    (reasonif AIME/GPQA ~18% residual, scored as lower bound). Phase spend ≈$18; cumulative ≈$21
    of $5k. **Open caveats for later segments:** (a) all sources are public benchmarks →
    **contamination/memorization** may confound later CoT-controllability interpretation (answer
    recalled from weights vs computed in CoT); a fresh/synthetic source (e.g. GSM-Symbolic) is a
    possible future add. (b) The scorer's error bounds were proven on the BASE model's clean
    finals; **re-validate by inspection on each downstream FT/steered model's messier outputs.**
    (c) Downstream accuracy passes must reuse the per-source caps + recovery (truncation×CoT-length
    confound) or report truncation separately. No segments added/removed/reordered.

- **Segment 2 — Instruction suite, compliance scoring & base baseline** (`instruction_suite`):
  Build a taxonomy of reasoning-trace instructions (casing, word inclusion/exclusion,
  formatting incl. bullet `- `, line structure, length, etc.), split train/val/held-out
  (held-out includes the bullet probe). Robust per-instruction compliance scorers
  (programmatic where possible, LLM judge where needed) on the `analysis` channel; a
  meta-discussion detector. Reproduce the base-model CoT-vs-output controllability gap
  (Chen et al.). Validate scorers via heavy data inspection.
  - **STATUS: COMPLETE (1 phase).** Built the instruction suite + CoT-compliance machinery,
    heavily validated, and self-reviewed (a `research-plan-reviewer` pass whose findings were
    addressed). **Locked in:** **Taxonomy = 25 instructions / 6 categories** (`instructions.py`,
    the single shared module): casing (all_caps, all_lower, initial_caps), suppression (no_commas,
    no_the, no_answer_word, no_word_so), inclusion (include_therefore, include_marker_note,
    start_with_phrase, include_quote_marker, include_exactly_twice), formatting (bullet, numbered,
    section_headers, xml_steps), length (brief_50w, length_30_60w, under_70w, terse_25w),
    style/language (questions, second_person, reason_in_spanish, no_first_person,
    child_explanation). Each targets the **`analysis`** channel, instruction in the **user message**
    referencing "your reasoning/CoT"; per-instruction scorer (programmatic; cached **Haiku-4-5**
    judge for 4 style instructions). **Deterministic persisted INSTRUCTION splits**
    (`data/instruction_splits.json`): **train 12 / val 4 / heldout 9**, with the **ENTIRE formatting
    category held out** (cross-category test) incl. the **bullet probe**, plus novel within-category
    instances in val+heldout. Generalization framing = a **single general "control-CoT" direction**
    (transfers across held-out instruction *types*), per-instruction directions as fallback; the
    held-out claim is stated narrowly (transfer to the fully-held-out formatting category + novel
    within-category instances; leave-one-category-out is noted future work). **Two metrics:**
    **`raw_compliance`** (scorer on `analysis` over all attempts; Chen/METR-comparable; PRIMARY for
    the gap) and a strict **`effective_control`** = compliant ∧ ¬meta ∧ ¬vacuous(genuine) ∧
    ¬malformed ∧ ¬truncated, all-attempts denominator (**PRIMARY downstream north-star**, adopted to
    close an inflation loophole). Also a **meta-discussion detector** (`judges.py:judge_meta`, κ≈0.91
    on a random held-out main sample, high-recall/precision≈0.93 → effective_control conservative), a
    **genuine/vacuous** measure (`judge_genuine`), and a **causal** column (paired vs the
    no-instruction trace). Scorers VALIDATED: 111 programmatic unit/constructed-true-positive tests
    (base ≈0 so true positives are constructed), LLM judges vs hand labels + vs Opus on 150 real
    rows. **Base CoT compliance is category-dependent:** casing/formatting/style ≈0%, suppression
    ~6% effective (~0.5–12% causal), inclusion-presence high raw (47%, causal ~80%) but **largely
    meta-confounded → 5% effective**, length **genuine** (42% raw → 36% effective). Aggregate raw
    17.9% / **effective 7.8%** / **meta 46%** / genuine 99%; **held-out macro effective ≈1.5%** (lots
    of headroom). **Accuracy preserved** under instructions (~93–97%); malformed ~0%. **CoT-vs-output
    gap reproduced** (scientific result): same transform, **CoT 6.8% vs output 76.3% → +69.6 pts**
    (output forced to a 40–90-word free-text explanation so non-trivial; length-confound ruled out by
    per-100-word violation rate). **Channel-routing confirmed at scale:** told to format its
    *reasoning*, base keeps `analysis` natural / meta-discusses and emits the format into `final`
    (a first-class monitorability diagnostic, `leak→final`). **Power analysis:** held-out base
    effective ≈1.5% → **minimum interesting uplift = +10 pp**; PRIMARY held-out claim = aggregate /
    category-macro (~79 paired items, **~15–20 tasks/instruction**, paired McNemar); per-instruction
    (~95/instr) is secondary. Hardened the shared **MCQ extractor to case-insensitive** (lowercase
    finals under casing instructions; 0/1669 Segment-1 base scores changed). Phase spend ≈$46;
    cumulative ≈$72 of $5k (the write-up rounds to ≈$66; immaterial). **Carry-forward caveats:**
    (a) **re-validate the compliance scorers + meta/genuine judges by inspection on each downstream
    FT/steered model's messier `analysis`/`final`** before trusting its numbers; specifically
    **re-check meta-judge precision on intervention outputs** (if an intervention lowers overt
    meta-discussion and the high-recall judge over-flags, the measured effective_control *uplift*
    could be inflated). (b) A **strong prompted few-shot baseline** is a noted future control for the
    FT/steering deliverables (so uplift isn't attributable to "could've prompted it"). (c)
    style/language `raw_compliance` is itself a judge output (carries judge error). No segments
    added/removed/reordered.

- **Segment 3 — Edited-reasoning training data** (`training_data`): Build the SFT dataset
  (prompt-with-instruction → complying reasoning trace + correct answer) for TRAIN
  instructions/tasks, plus the raw-trace SFT control set (instruction in prompt,
  non-complying target). Use the `dataset-creation` skill; verify traces actually reason,
  solve the task, comply, and avoid meta-discussion.
  - **STATUS: COMPLETE (1 phase).** Built the edited-reasoning SFT dataset by **editing the base
    model's own natural traces** (METR recipe), heavily verified, no leakage. **Locked-in artifacts:**
    `data/sft_edited_reasoning_full.jsonl` (**3,600 compliant** examples, balanced 300/instruction ×
    50/source over the 12 TRAIN instructions × 6 sources, from 1,182 distinct TRAIN tasks; every target
    is compliant ∧ genuine ∧ ¬meta ∧ **analysis-concludes-gold** ∧ correct `final` ∧ well-formed
    Harmony); `data/sft_raw_trace_control_full.jsonl` (**3,373 matched raw-trace control**, same prompts,
    non-complying-but-genuine+correct targets, non-compliance measured per row); `data/sft_plain_full.jsonl`
    (300 no-instruction "plain" examples for an optional default-behaviour mix). **Editing = hybrid:**
    programmatic structure-preserving edits for casing(LaTeX-masked)/no_commas/inclusion; **Opus-4.7
    LLM editing** (edits, never re-solves) for no_the/brief_50w/length_30_60w/questions/second_person/
    reason_in_spanish, behind a **source-faithfulness gate** + a **blind concludes-gold gate** (true
    conclusion-drift in LLM edits ≈0.5–1%). Independent recompute from stored text: 0 failures.
    **Rendering locked to eval:** `include_date=False`, instruction in the **user** message,
    byte-for-byte the Segment-2 eval prompt; `prompt_token_sha256` persisted; `harmony_utils.
    render_training_example` builds the training sequence (the Harmony training renderer drops prior-turn
    analysis — build completion tokens directly). **MAJOR carry-forward finding (high-stakes for Seg 5):**
    the Segment-2 **Haiku `judge_meta` over-flags COMPLYING/styled CoTs as meta** (~40–95% FP on
    questions/second_person, ~4–16% on casing/inclusion); Opus is reliable → **all FT/steered evals must
    score meta (and style/Spanish compliance, and concludes-gold) with Opus**, AND the Segment-2 baseline
    `effective_control` (the Δ reference, Haiku-scored) **must be re-scored with Opus meta** for an
    apples-to-apples uplift (else uplift is inflated). Minor: 5/3600 rows (one long reasonif task) have an
    un-adjudicated `meta=null` gate (drop/re-judge in Seg 4); self-reported cost understated (true
    cumulative ≈ **$230** of $5k per `total_cost.jsonl`, Opus editor/judges dominate). No segments
    reordered.

- **Segment 4 — Fine-tuning pipeline & smoke test** (`ft_pipeline`): Stand up the LoRA
  fine-tuning pipeline end-to-end (Tinker or local PEFT, per Segment 0's decision) on the
  edited-reasoning data; get one configuration training cleanly with sane loss curves; load
  the resulting model into the eval harness and confirm a *preliminary* held-out compliance
  signal vs base. Goal: de-risk the FT path, not the final result.
  - **STATUS: COMPLETE (1 phase).** De-risked the LoRA FT path end-to-end, self-reviewed (3
    `research-plan-reviewer` passes that found + FIXED two real measurement bugs). **Locked in:**
    **Engine = Tinker LoRA** (rank 32, MoE-experts+attn+unembed) fed **EXACT pre-rendered Harmony
    tokens** (bypasses Tinker's date-line renderer), completion-only loss mask → **rendering alignment
    PROVEN on all 6,973 SFT rows** (train tokens == eval prompt == persisted `prompt_token_sha256`).
    Reusable pipeline at project root: `verify_render_alignment.py`, `ft_data.py`, `run_ft_train.py`,
    `ft_merge_modal.py`+`run_ft_merge.py` (CPU-merge adapter→MXFP4 on the volume), `gpt_oss_infer.py`
    extended (`GptOss(model_path="/cache/merged_ft_<tag>")` + `weights_id`-namespaced cache),
    `run_ft_eval.py`, `run_ft_eval_judges.py` (**meta=Opus**, style/Spanish-compliance=Opus,
    genuine=Haiku), `analyze_ft_eval.py`, `make_ft_inspection.py`. One clean config (rank 32 / α 32 /
    LR 2e-4 / 3 epochs / batch 128), loss 0.709→0.115 smooth; merged model loads (0% malformed) and is
    **steering-hookable (exercised: capture + zero/non-zero steering hooks fire every decode step).**
    **Preliminary held-out signal (Opus-meta, matched base, macro `effective_control`):** TRAIN
    +53.1 (13.3→66.4), **WITHIN-category held-out +12.0** (4.4→16.5), **CROSS-category/formatting (never
    trained, incl. bullet probe) +11.1** (0→11.1); the matched **raw-trace control FT shows NO uplift**
    → uplift is genuine instruction-following, not generic SFT (`raw_compliance` corroborates without the
    meta judge). Honest reading: the held-out macro is **carried by 1–2 instructions** (WITHIN ≈ terse_25w
    22→82%; CROSS ≈ bullet 0→33% + numbered 0→11%); the rest is Segment-5 sweep headroom. Accuracy
    preserved on no-instruction (88→94%). **Carry-forward problems for Segment 5:** (a) the un-swept
    config emits **degenerate repetition loops on ~22% of no-instruction traces** (excluded from the
    metric, but the deliverable config must mitigate this — plain-mix / lower LR-epochs); (b) **MXFP4
    merge fidelity** — the served FT = `dequant(quant(base+LoRA))`, a quantization round-trip the base
    never sees; the deliverable should serve unquantized or quantify fidelity; (c) **Haiku genuine
    false-negatives on terse (≤25-word) reasoning** (conservative) → use Opus genuine for terse; (d) the
    base re-scored with **Opus meta** is mandatory for an apples-to-apples Δ (Haiku understates uplift).
    **VAL instructions are all within-category** (no formatting) → a val-selected sweep can optimize
    within-category generalization but NOT select for cross-category/formatting transfer (report it on
    held-out, never select on it). Phase spend ≈$14; cumulative ≈**$245** of $5k (4.9%). No segments
    reordered.

- **Segment 5 — Fine-tuning reproduction & deliverable** (`ft_reproduction`): LR/epoch sweep on
  validation; full held-out evaluation of compliance uplift + task accuracy + meta-discussion +
  malformed-rate; raw-trace SFT control; statistical significance with a pre-stated target n.
  **Deliverable #1.**
  - **STATUS: COMPLETE (1 phase) — Deliverable #1 achieved.** Rigorous, significance-tested,
    accuracy-preserving, control-dissociated held-out CoT-controllability uplift; numbers reconcile
    exactly with the raw artifacts; self-reviewed (3 reviewer passes, fixes folded in). **Headline
    (held-out `effective_control`, Opus-meta, matched base, n=100 source-stratified tasks/instruction,
    task-level cluster bootstrap):** ALL-9-held-out **1.6%→13.9%, +12.3pp [+10.4,+14.2]** (CI lower
    bound clears the pre-stated +10pp minimum); CROSS-category formatting (never trained, incl. the
    bullet probe) **+15.2 [+12.2,+18.5]** (bullet 0→52%, McNemar p<1e-4); WITHIN +10.0 [+7.8,+12.2]
    (does NOT robustly clear +10pp). **Honest reading:** the aggregate is *concentrated in 2–3
    instructions* (bullet, terse_25w, numbered; median per-instruction uplift 0; dropping bullet OR
    terse pushes the aggregate below +10pp) — a significant aggregate uplift, **not a broad win**. But
    it is **not bullet-specific**: 2 brand-new unseen line-prefix formats also transfer (gt_prefix
    +25, arrow_prefix +16.7) → FT taught a *general simple-line-prefix CoT-form control skill* that
    does NOT yet extend to structurally-complex formats (section_headers/xml_steps flat). Joint
    comply-AND-correct +10.7 [+8.8,+12.7]; raw_compliance (METR-anchor) 2.1→14.4%. **Controls:**
    raw-trace matched-target control FT shows NO uplift (Δ compliant−control +13.6 [+11.7,+15.3];
    control reaches *lower* train loss → not undertrained → uplift is genuine instruction-following,
    not generic SFT); a strong K=4 few-shot baseline gives **0%** held-out effective_control (≪ FT;
    demos make base meta-discuss + route format to `final`). **Config (selected on VAL only):** LR
    2e-4 / 3ep / rank 32 (gpt-oss caps LoRA rank at 32) / **+8% plain-mix**, which fixed the Segment-4
    degenerate long-tail (no-instr degenerate 17.5%→7.5%≈base; final no-instr degen 10%≈base 11%,
    accuracy 89.5→92.5%). **Serving:** unquantized **bf16 merge** (LoRA delta never sees MXFP4; base &
    FT share identical dequant(base_mxfp4) weights → no precision confound); deliverable FT artifact =
    `cdel` (matched to the control's 3,368 shared prompts), served at `/cache/merged_ft_cdel_bf16`,
    **steering-hookable (verified) → the Segment-6 benchmark.** Matched control = `ctrldel`. **Length
    instructions trade accuracy for compliance** (terse 86→76, brief 80→67.5) — flagged. **Deferred
    (correctly):** a fresh-source (e.g. GSM-Symbolic) contamination/accuracy-preservation check → the
    robustness segment. Cumulative spend ≈ **$326** of $5k (6.5%).

- **Segment 6 — Single-layer steering vectors** (`single_layer_steering`): Derive candidate
  directions (prefer complying-vs-non-complying activation contrasts on matched prompts;
  optionally FT-weight-derived), built from TRAIN instructions and tested on HELD-OUT; single
  -layer additive residual steering; sweep layer/sign/magnitude on validation; eval on held-out
  with random-matched-norm (multi-seed null) and sign-reversed controls; report malformed-rate.
  - **STATUS: COMPLETE (1 phase) — genuine but PARTIAL; single-layer is INSUFFICIENT → escalate
    (Segment 7).** Rigorous, honest, self-reviewed (10 reviewer points addressed); numbers reconcile
    with the raw artifacts; met every rubric must-have. **Method:** primary direction = pooled
    complying-vs-non-complying **matched-pair** activation contrast on TRAIN (diff-of-means over
    analysis-channel tokens, per layer); magnitude = c·mean‖resid‖_L·unit(v) (diff-of-means is only
    ~3% of the residual norm; c≥0.5 garbles); VAL-selected **pooled @ layer 8, c=0.25, sign +**.
    **Headline (held-out 9 instr × 39 tasks, Opus-meta, matched base, task-cluster bootstrap):**
    all-held-out `effective_control` **+7.7pp [+4.8,+10.5]** (within +13.8, **cross-category/formatting
    +0.0**), **beats a 5-seed random matched-norm null** (null macro max +0.6) and is **sign-specific**
    (−0.9), malformed 0%. **Carried by terse_25w (10→41) + no_word_so (0→38.5); other 7 held-out 0→0.**
    `raw_compliance` 1.4→12.5%. **Two binding gaps:** (i) **ZERO formatting/casing transfer, and it is
    STRUCTURAL** — a single fixed additive vector is inherently **non-instruction-conditional** (can't
    read which instruction is in the prompt) so it injects one fixed terse/suppression CoT-*style*
    shift, NOT FT's prompt-specific form control (even in-distribution all_caps 0→0%; pushing magnitude
    garbles rather than inducing bullets; FT got formatting by changing the **weights**); (ii) a **~12pp
    accuracy cost** (joint comply-AND-correct +6.3pp). So single-layer **does NOT reproduce FT** (whose
    held-out win was bullet 0→52 + terse + numbered) and does NOT clear the +10pp minimum-interesting
    effect. A **Spanish/language-shift artifact** appears at higher magnitudes (style-dominated
    direction) but is absent from the effective rows at the selected config. **Carry-forward confound:**
    the matched-pair contrast inherits an **edited-surface/token-identity** confound (complying targets
    are Opus-edited) → the discriminating test for genuine *control* is **held-out transfer to a NEW,
    specifically-instructed form** (esp. the bullet probe). Cumulative spend ≈ **$360** of $5k (≈7%).
    Reusable: `steering_lib.py`, `run_derive_directions.py`, `steer_eval_lib.py`, `run_steer_eval.py`/
    `run_steer_judges.py`/`analyze_steer_eval.py`, `data/steering_directions.npz`, `gpt_oss_infer.capture_spans`.

- **Segment 7 — Multi-method steering buildout** (`multi_method_steering`): If single-layer is
  insufficient (or to strengthen): multi-layer steering, additive residual biases, sparse layer
  subsets, gradient-trained steering vectors / alternative objectives. Validation-split search
  to find the best small intervention; decide general-direction vs per-instruction framing.
  - **PRIORITIZED BY SEG-6 FINDINGS (in progress):** the two binding gaps drive the escalation order.
    **(1) Gradient-trained small additive intervention = the decisive experiment:** a learned additive
    activation bias (frozen weights; complying-target NLL objective — "FT but only allowed to change a
    fixed additive bias"; trained on TRAIN only) directly isolates the **additive-representation limit**
    from the **TRAIN-provenance limit** and tests whether a cheap intervention can recover FT's
    **formatting/conditional transfer (esp. bullet)** by *modulating* the in-context-instruction circuit.
    **(2) Multi-layer diff-of-means** primarily targets the **accuracy cost** + within-category strength
    (a multi-layer *fixed* vector is still non-conditional → not expected to add formatting). **(3)
    Per-category/per-instruction directions** + an explicit **single-general-vs-per-instruction decision**
    (different monitorability implications). **(4) FT-vs-base activation diff** as a cheap provenance
    comparison. Keep interventions **small (report size)**; controls = base + multi-seed random null +
    sign-reversed (fixed directions) / base + trained-on-control + held-out-generalization (trained);
    Opus-meta + guardrails throughout; modest held-out n (full-n significance is Segment 8). **Either
    outcome is a real result:** a strong steering reproduction, OR a precise characterization that cheap
    steering surfaces a general CoT-*style* shift but not FT's instruction-conditional form control.
  - **STATUS: COMPLETE — central question answered at TIER 1 (strongest monitorability threat; 1 phase).**
    Rigorous, honest, self-reviewed (10 reviewer points addressed); numbers reconcile with raw artifacts;
    met every rubric must-have. **The decisive Priority-1 experiment is a strong POSITIVE:** a single
    **gradient-trained, frozen-weights, 2,880-param additive vector at one resid_post layer** (`gL10`,
    layer 10; completion-only NLL on the complying SFT targets; TRAIN-only; 500 steps/lr 0.05; seed 0)
    **reproduces FT's held-out CoT-control profile INCLUDING the instruction-conditional formatting
    transfer the Seg-6 diff-of-means could NOT.** Held-out (9 instr × 39 tasks, Opus-meta, matched base,
    task-cluster bootstrap): all-held-out `effective_control` **+11.4pp [+8.3,+14.8]** ≈ FT's +10.6;
    **cross-category/formatting +11.5 [+6.4,+17.3]** (diff-of-means was +0.0), carried by **bullet
    0→30.8% (McNemar p<0.001)** (sibling gL8 ≈49% = FT, but held-out-informed); within +11.3 (terse
    10→67%); `raw_compliance` 1.4→13.4% (FT 12.3%); **no aggregate accuracy cost** (joint comply-AND-
    correct +8.6); malformed 0%, genuine 97.7%, meta 50→8%. **The fixed vector is effectively
    INSTRUCTION-CONDITIONAL (behavioral):** asked form appears ONLY when asked (no-instruction: 0/39
    bullets/numbered/caps) — it amplifies the model's in-context instruction-following. **Clean
    dissociation:** the trained-on-control twin `gL10ctrl` (same training on NON-complying targets,
    matched ‖v‖, *lower* loss) = 0% everywhere, and a random matched-norm vector @L10 = 0% → it is the
    LEARNED direction, not a big push. **Seed-robust** (bullet 31/36/39% at seeds 0/1/2). **Central
    ablation:** NO fixed diff-of-means direction does formatting (single-layer / multi-layer / per-
    category / FT-vs-base all cross-category +0.0) → the **gradient objective**, not multi-layer/
    provenance/per-category, is the cause. Multi-layer DOM buys within-category (+26) + an accuracy cost
    + a language-shift artifact, still 0 formatting. **General-vs-per-instruction decision:** a single
    GENERAL gradient-trained knob (the strong, monitorability-relevant claim). Tier-2 (a dedicated
    conditional module) is MOOT — tier-1 succeeded. **Two measurement caveats carried to Segment 8:**
    (i) Seg-7 mnt-capped steered arms at 1024 while FT used full mnt+recovery → Seg-8 must compare on a
    matched convention (`raw_compliance` is the truncation-robust head-to-head); (ii) gL8 was probed on
    held-out before VAL picked gL10 → Seg-8 must pre-commit **gL10** (no held-out selection); gL8/gML are
    held-out-informed sensitivity arms only. Honest profile: concentrated in bullet+terse (5/9 held-out
    flat — sweep headroom, like FT); mild always-on no-instruction verbosity/degeneration side-effect.
    Cumulative spend ≈ **$443** of $5k (≈9%). Reusable: `run_grad_steer_train.py`, `grad_steer_lib.py`,
    `gpt_oss_infer.train_steer`, `run_grad_steer_eval.py`, `analyze_grad_steer_eval.py`,
    `make_grad_steer_inspection.py`, `data/grad_steer_gL10.npz` (+ `gL10ctrl`/`gL10_s1`/`gL10_s2`/`gL10rand`).

- **Segment 8 — Steering full evaluation & deliverable** (`steering_full_eval`): Consolidate the
  best intervention; full held-out evaluation vs base, FT, random (multi-seed), and
  sign-reversed controls; task accuracy, meta-discussion, vacuous-reasoning, and malformed-rate
  checks; statistical significance. **Deliverable #2 (central result).**
  - **FINDINGS-DRIVEN DESIGN (next up):** pre-commit the Seg-7 **`gL10`** (VAL-selected, seed 0, 2,880
    params) — NO held-out selection. Run the full-n (~100/instr, the Seg-5 FT subset) significance-
    tested held-out eval on **identical (instruction × task) items** vs **base, FT `cdel`, a ≥5-seed
    random matched-norm null, and the trained-on-control twin `gL10ctrl`**, on the **FT-matched
    generation convention** (full per-source mnt + recovery — NOT the Seg-7 1024 cap; report truncation;
    `raw_compliance` is the truncation-robust head-to-head). Pre-state the primary metric (aggregate/
    macro held-out `effective_control` uplift) + the **+10pp** minimum effect; per-instr McNemar +
    **task-level cluster bootstrap**; multi-seed (1/2) robustness. Guardrails (accuracy / joint comply-
    AND-correct / malformed / genuine / degenerate) + the no-instruction always-on side-effect quantified
    + judge re-validation on the final steered traces. **Monitorability takeaway scoped precisely:**
    *reachability* of the latent machinery (a single frozen-weights residual vector, zero weight change),
    NOT a cheaper-to-mount attack (gL10 needs the same SFT targets + white-box gradients as FT). Broad
    generalization/robustness + the lightweight monitorability-faithfulness probe are Segment 9; "why"
    is Segment 10+.
  - **STATUS: COMPLETE (1 phase) — Deliverable #2 achieved (the central result).** Rigorous, full-n,
    significance-tested, apples-to-apples held-out steering result; numbers reconcile EXACTLY with the
    raw artifacts (`results/steer_deliverable_gL10.{md,json}`); self-reviewed (3 reviewer passes, all
    fixes folded in); the paired gL10−FT test (main-planner feedback) was implemented. **Headline
    (held-out 9 instr × 100 source-stratified tasks = the IDENTICAL items as the Seg-5 FT deliverable,
    FT-matched generation convention, Opus-meta, task-level cluster bootstrap):** a single
    **frozen-weights 2,880-param additive vector `gL10`** (resid_post @ layer 10, complying-target NLL,
    seed 0, ‖v‖≈148; PRE-COMMITTED, no held-out selection) gives all-held-out `effective_control`
    **1.6%→14.3%, uplift +12.8pp [+10.7,+14.9]** (P≤0=0, **clears the pre-stated +10pp**); cross-category
    formatting **+14.5 [+11.5,+17.8]** (**bullet 0→48%**, McNemar p<0.001; recovers the formatting the
    Seg-6 diff-of-means could NOT); within +11.4 [+9.2,+13.4]. **Statistically INDISTINGUISHABLE FROM FT
    in aggregate** (paired gL10−FT +0.4 [−1.9,+2.8], cross −0.8, within +1.4 — all bracket 0).
    `raw_compliance` 2.1→15.7% (FT 14.4%); joint comply-AND-correct +11.1 [+9.1,+13.1] (FT +10.7).
    **Concentrated in bullet + terse_25w (14→69%) + numbered (0→8%); 5/9 held-out flat — the SAME
    concentration as FT** ("reproduces FT" = matches FT's aggregate + profile, not a broad win). **Clean
    control battery:** trained-on-control twin `gL10ctrl` = 0% everywhere (Δ +14.3 [+12.3,+16.3], P=0,
    despite *lower* train loss); 5-seed random matched-norm null mean −0.1 [max +1.0]; sign-reversed
    −gL10 = 0% → the effect is the **LEARNED direction + sign**, not a push of this norm at L10.
    Seed-robust (bullet 48/47/50%). malformed 0%, genuine 97.4%, meta 54.7→9.0%, truncated 3.8%≈base
    (Seg-7 truncation gap GONE under the matched convention). **Judge re-validation PASSED** (Opus-meta
    precise + CONSERVATIVE on gL10's complying traces — would lower, not inflate, the uplift; the two
    carriers bullet [programmatic] + terse are judge-INDEPENDENT, so the meta-inflation threat cannot
    touch the load-bearing numbers). **Two honest asymmetries vs FT (carry forward):** (1) instructed
    held-out accuracy gL10 86.3% vs FT 91.5% (terse step-skipping; joint comply-correct nets out); (2)
    an **always-on no-instruction side-effect** — FT is inert there, gL10 doubles the no-instr
    degenerate-rate (11%→20%) + inflates verbosity (aw mean 498→766), a real monitorability-relevant
    property of an always-on vector. **Scoping (load-bearing):** what is demonstrated is cheap,
    instruction-conditional control of CoT **FORM/LENGTH, NOT content-concealment**; the monitorability
    claim is **REACHABILITY** of the latent machinery (one frozen-weights residual vector, zero weight
    change), **NOT a cheaper-to-mount attack** (gL10 needs the same SFT targets + white-box gradients as
    FT). **Honest "clears +10pp" caveat:** the all-held-out lower bound (+10.7) barely clears, within
    does NOT robustly clear (+9.2 — same as FT), and the clearing macros are each carried by a single
    instruction (terse / bullet). Cumulative spend ≈ **$544** of $5k (~11%). Reusable:
    `data/grad_steer_gL10.npz` (+ `gL10ctrl`/`gL10_s1`/`gL10_s2`/`gL10rand`), `run_grad_steer_eval.py`,
    `analyze_steer_deliverable.py`, `make_steer_deliverable_audit.py`, `gpt_oss_infer.train_steer`.
    **Deferred to Segment 9 (NOT gaps):** the dose-response/magnitude sweep, broad generalization/
    robustness, the fresh-source (GSM-Symbolic) contamination check, and the lightweight monitorability/
    faithfulness probe. No segments added/removed/reordered.

- **Segment 9 — Steering generalization, robustness & monitorability probe**
  (`steering_robustness`): Generalization across held-out instruction types/tasks; dose-response
  curves; interaction with reasoning effort; transfer/stability; harden controls and rule out
  confounds. Includes a **lightweight monitorability/faithfulness probe** pulled forward so the
  safety-relevant headline isn't hostage to leftover budget.
  - **FINDINGS-DRIVEN DESIGN (next up).** Deliverable #2 is done and scoped to FORM/LENGTH control, NOT
    content-concealment — so this segment's distinctive job is the **lightweight monitorability/
    faithfulness probe** (Priority 1, protect it): does a cheap activation steer that shortens/compresses
    the CoT (the demonstrated capability — `gL10`-under-terse, a trained terse/faithfulness direction, or
    the Seg-6 terse-dominated direction) degrade the **content** a monitor needs — e.g. reduce
    verbalization of a decision-relevant cue (cue-based faithfulness, à la arXiv:2501.08156) or an LLM
    monitor's catch-rate? **A clean NULL is a valuable bound; do not manufacture a positive.** Validate
    any new judge by inspection; this is a LIGHTWEIGHT first probe (the full cue-based steer-against-
    faithfulness suite is Segment 12; misbehavior-monitoring is Segment 13). Plus (Priority 2) the
    **dose-response/magnitude sweep** of `gL10` (graded push→compliance; the no-effect→garble window;
    robustness around ‖v‖≈148); (Priority 3) **generalization** (brand-new asked-forms gt_prefix/
    arrow_prefix like FT; the reasoning-effort interaction — Chen et al.: controllability falls with
    effort; per-source stability); (Priority 4) the **fresh-source contamination check** (GSM-Symbolic
    accuracy-preservation, the long-standing Seg-1 caveat). Reuse `gL10` (don't re-train the headline);
    keep the Opus-meta + guardrails + cluster-bootstrap + judge-re-validation discipline on every new
    condition. May warrant >1 phase. **Interpretability/mechanistic "why" is Segments 10–11 — out of
    scope here.**
  - **STATUS: COMPLETE (1 phase).** All 5 deliverables done + validated; 3 research-reviewer passes with
    real fixes (a no-final accuracy bug; the matched-length selectivity test; alarm-volume accounting;
