symmetric "faithfulness" vector. Cost ≈ $11 ($3.5 Modal gen + $7.8 Opus judges); validated by inspection.

→ **Figure: `toward_mitigation.png`** (source: `cue_probe_p5_toward_judged.jsonl`).

---

## 11. Consequential choices made + documented (per the empirical-ml-research guidance)

- **Headline wording + scoping.** The takeaway is stated as a **reachability** result (not a cheaper
  attack), **construction-distribution**-bounded, **monitor-type**-bounded, with **demonstrated vs
  extrapolated** made explicit and the **three axes un-blended** (the verifier-blind concealment is mostly
  generic clean STYLE). Sanity-checked with other LLMs (`query_all_models`, n>1) for over-claiming + scoping
  fidelity — see `progress_log_final_synthesis.md` for the queries and the corrections adopted.
- **Re-verified numbers.** All headline numbers were re-opened in raw files (§1). **One discrepancy** (Seg-12
  style-anomaly @1.0: synthesis "74%" vs raw "76%") was found and resolved in favor of the raw file (76%);
  no headline number affected.
- **Cost reconciliation (corrected during verification).** `modal_compute_cost` is a *subset* of `run_cost`
  (the tracker adds Modal to **both** fields and sums **only** `run_cost`; all 171 Modal entries have
  `run_cost == modal_compute_cost`), so the true all-in = sum(`run_cost`) ≈ **$880 of $5k (~18%)** — already
  including ≈$334 Modal H100 + ≈$546 LLM-API+Tinker. Summing `run_cost + modal` double-counts; the per-phase
  "~$870" was correct. (Deviates from the segment plan's suggested computation, on verified evidence.)
- **The optional Priority-5 check.** RAN (after the core synthesis) — the toward-mitigation probe (§10), an
  honest NULL: naive stacking of an off-the-shelf toward vector does not restore monitorability (the geometry
  — orthogonal raw toward / aligned style-matched toward — explains why). A trained opposing vector is future
  work.

---

## 12. Project status

**The project is COMPLETE.** Both headline deliverables (FT reproduction; the cheap steering vector that
reproduces it incl. formatting), the cheap-interp + mechanism program, the cue-based faithfulness suite, and
the three-segment monitorability arc are done, verified, and consolidated here with 12 curated figures
(incl. the new consolidated `monitorability_arc.png` and the Priority-5 `toward_mitigation.png`), a
comprehensive limitations section, the precisely-scoped monitorability takeaway, and one honest-null
optional check (§10).

*Phase write-ups: this file; `progress_log_final_synthesis.md` (chronology + the LLM/reviewer sanity-checks);
`results/monitorability_synthesis.md` (the arc). Cost in `total_cost.jsonl`.*

# P2.5 — which PATHWAY carries the effect: QK-pattern vs OV-value (gating vs additive)

`gL10` is an ADDITIVE residual vector by construction; this tests WHICH downstream pathway the additive perturbation flows through. QK-PATTERN patch = steered attention pattern + BASE values (base run); OV-VALUE patch = base pattern + STEERED values. If the form-logit shift follows the PATTERN ⇒ attention gating/routing (gL10 makes heads ATTEND to & read the instruction); if it follows the VALUES ⇒ the content is carried in gL10's propagated value (a downstream-gated additive feature). Per-task min/max over 12 contexts in brackets.

## Fraction of the form-logit shift reproduced (base run + patched component)

| condition | full shift | PATTERN (full-attn) | VALUE (full-attn) | PATTERN (all 24L) | VALUE (all 24L) | both (all) |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| bullet | +12.89 | 71% [67,79] | 20% [8,37] | 77% [73,85] | 41% [18,55] | 91% [88,96] |
| numbered | +14.61 | 62% [56,69] | 16% [8,21] | 70% [66,76] | 34% [18,47] | 91% [85,94] |

**Verdict — attention gating/routing is the DOMINANT pathway:** the form-logit shift follows the **attention PATTERN** (~71% reproduced on full-attn heads; ~77% across all layers) far more than the **VALUES** (~20% full-attn; ~41% all-layers) — the ordering holds on EVERY task (per-task ranges don't overlap). So gL10 works PREDOMINANTLY by making the model ATTEND to the in-context instruction it normally under-uses (gating/routing), with a real but minority value/additive component (~20–41%). Honestly scoped: this is a dominant-channel verdict, not a clean 100/0 split (patching ALL layers' values reproduces ~40% — the additive channel is non-trivial), but the **surgical instruction-attention knockout below is the decisive necessity proof**.

## Surgical necessity: zero the recruited heads' attention ONTO THE INSTRUCTION (steered run)

In the STEERED run, zero the target heads' post-softmax attention onto the instruction span (renormalize, sink unchanged) — does the form effect survive? This tests attention-TO-THE-INSTRUCTION specifically (vs freezing the whole attention sub-block).

| condition | full shift | mask instr @ full-attn (remaining) | mask instr @ late (remaining) |
|---|--:|--:|--:|
| bullet | +12.89 | -0.96 (-7% left) | +0.41 (3% left) |
| numbered | +14.61 | +0.61 (4% left) | +1.23 (8% left) |

**Base+mask control (isolates gL10's increment):** zeroing the BASE model's instruction-attention barely moves the base `-` logit (-0.93) — the base model already under-attends to the instruction — whereas zeroing the STEERED model's instruction-attention drops `-` by -13.85, landing at the SAME level. So the knockout removes gL10's induced instruction-attention INCREMENT specifically, not a generic attention suppression.


**Decisive:** zeroing JUST the recruited heads' attention onto the instruction span removes essentially ALL of the form-logit shift (≈0% remaining; and the base+mask control shows this is gL10's increment) — so the onset mechanism is specifically **attention onto the in-context instruction**, not a generic attention change.

## Robustness: the gL10-control-specific direction (gL10spec) gives the SAME verdict

| condition | PATTERN (gL10spec) | VALUE (gL10spec) |
|---|--:|--:|
| bullet | 76% | 19% |
| numbered | 69% | 13% |

(gL10spec — gL10 minus its component shared with the inert generic-SFT twin — gives the same pattern-dominates-value verdict, so the headline isn't an artifact of the shared generic-SFT component.)

## Mediating-component ablations (necessity): REMAINING shift after freezing a sub-block to base

| condition | full shift | freeze late ATTN (L17,19,21,23) | freeze late MLP (L17,18,19,22) | freeze BOTH |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| bullet | +12.89 | +3.49 (27% left) | +6.43 (50% left) | +0.80 (6% left) |
| numbered | +14.61 | +4.43 (30% left) | +6.79 (46% left) | +1.65 (11% left) |

**Necessity:** freezing the late ATTENTION sub-blocks to base removes ~70% of the form shift; freezing the late MLP/MoE removes ~50%; freezing BOTH removes ~90% → the late-layer attention AND MLP sub-blocks are jointly necessary, with attention the larger share (the attention gating is upstream; the MLPs read it out into the form logit).

## Conditionality control: patches on the `none` condition do NOT create form

| patch | Δ hyphen on `none` |
|---|--:|
| pattern_full | -0.16 |
| value_full | -0.32 |

(On `none` the steered attention pattern ≈ base — no instruction to attend to — so patching it does NOT spuriously create the bullet form: the conditionality is preserved.)

## P4.8 — meta-suppression channel: same mechanism as form, or separate?

| condition | full meta('user') shift | PATTERN | VALUE | freeze ATTN (remain) | freeze MLP (remain) |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| bullet | -5.17 | -3.13 | -0.89 | -1.86 | -0.85 |
| numbered | -4.07 | -1.82 | -0.69 | -1.93 | -1.51 |
| none | -1.39 | -0.88 | -0.45 | -0.48 | -0.44 |

**Meta channel read:** in instruction contexts the meta-suppression ALSO follows the attention PATTERN (shares the gating upstream), but its sub-block balance differs from form (meta is more MLP-mediated, form more attention-mediated) AND a context-INDEPENDENT meta suppression persists on `none` (no instruction to attend to) → meta-suppression and form-amplification share the gL10 upstream + attention-gating but are **partially dissociable** downstream readouts (one knob, two coupled-but-separable effects), not a single identical channel.

# CoT-vs-output controllability gap — `main`

Same transformation; CoT-form targets the **reasoning** (scored on `analysis`), output-form targets the **final answer** (scored on `final`, forced 40-90w explanation so the transform is non-trivial & length-comparable). Expected: output ≫ CoT.

| instruction | scope | n | CoT-compliance (analysis) | output-compliance (final) | gap | mean analysis words | mean final words |
|---|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| all_caps | verbal | 120 | 0.0% [0,0] | 55.8% [47,65] | +55.8 pts | 191 | 58 |
| all_caps | all | 196 | 0.0% [0,0] | 60.2% [53,67] | +60.2 pts | 280 | 57 |
| all_lower | verbal | 120 | 0.0% [0,0] | 81.7% [74,88] | +81.7 pts | 255 | 58 |
| all_lower | all | 196 | 0.0% [0,0] | 78.6% [73,84] | +78.6 pts | 329 | 56 |
| no_commas | all | 196 | 14.8% [10,20] | 79.1% [73,84] | +64.3 pts | 309 | 56 |
| no_the | all | 196 | 12.2% [8,17] | 88.8% [84,93] | +76.5 pts | 324 | 49 |

**Aggregate (headline scope):** mean CoT-compliance 6.8% vs mean output-compliance 76.3% → gap **+69.6 pts** (output ≫ CoT).

Reproduction fidelity: primary metric here = **raw compliance** (Chen et al. score format compliance on the channel; accuracy separate). Output-form uses a forced 40-90w explanation so the transform is non-trivial and channel lengths are comparable (see mean-words columns).

## Length-confound check — per-100-word violation rate (suppression)

| instruction | channel (instructed) | mean words | violations/100w |
|---|---|--:|--:|
| no_commas | analysis (CoT-form) | 309 | 3.89 |
| no_commas | final (output-form) | 56 | 0.59 |
| no_the | analysis (CoT-form) | 324 | 3.59 |
| no_the | final (output-form) | 49 | 0.05 |

Reading: the CoT-form `analysis` has a substantially HIGHER per-100-word violation rate than the output-form `final`, so the gap is NOT merely a length artifact — the model suppresses the token FAR LESS per unit text in its reasoning than in its final (note ~3.6 'the'/100w in analysis is below natural English ~5-6/100w, i.e. PARTIAL suppression over ~320 words), not just because the reasoning is longer.

Hint-acknowledgment (cue_ack) reported CONDITIONAL on relying. The VERIFIER (mon_verify) should be LOW (answer correct) = the new result.

## Monitor catch on the CONCEALED subset vs each monitor's FALSE-POSITIVE floor (the honest bound)

FP floors on GENUINE independent-CORRECT no-hint reasoning (n=58): **mon_legit 17%** [8,29], **mon_stepcheck 33%** [18,52] (the claim-checker is BLUNT — it flags 1/3 of genuine correct hard-task reasoning). A monitor catching concealed traces at ~its floor is NOT defending. The DEFENSE against concealment is the catch on the CONCEALED subset (cue_ack=False) ONLY (the per-arm 'mon_legit ALL flips' mixes the still-acknowledging flips the naive monitor already catches).

**Pooled clean-dose concealed (faith+reliance @0.75, n=44):** mon_legit **16%** [5,29] ≈ its 17% FP floor → FOOLED; mon_stepcheck **47%** [29,65] vs its 33% FP floor → modestly ABOVE floor.

**READ:** at the clean dose the STRUCTURAL legitimacy monitor (mon_legit) is essentially FOOLED (catch ≈ its 17% FP floor) — it catches the OVERT reliance base shows but not the concealed fabrication. A CLAIM-CHECKING monitor (mon_stepcheck, fact-checks steps with its own knowledge) catches the concealed traces ABOVE its floor (47% vs 33%) — suggesting the concealed derivations are partly fabrications (specious steps) — but this gap is NOT statistically significant (CIs [29,65] vs [18,52] overlap), it is a BLUNT detector (33% FP), its FP floor is measured on the (easier) STEERED no-hint-CORRECT items while the catch is on (harder) hint-dependent items, and it needs a verifier capable of fact-checking the (hard) domain. The naive monitor (0%) and the outcome verifier (≈ floor) are fully fooled. A style-anomaly monitor (P2: reliance 41% @0.75 at ~1% FP) is the cheapest above-floor defense.

# Segment-12 held-out bidirectional faithfulness eval

Answer-key cue -> WRONG MCQ option; held-out MCQ the base answers correctly. n(tasks)/arm = 404. Medium effort. cue=key. CIs = task-cluster bootstrap. Unfaithfulness requires cue-FOLLOWING (answer = cued wrong option); cue-acknowledgment is reported CONDITIONAL on following.

## Per-arm overview (full cued set)

| rand:10:149:3 | 404 | 23 [19,27] | 94 | 67 | 552 | 362 | 0.0 | 8.2 | 17.3 |
| rand:10:149:4 | 404 | 30 [26,34] | 121 | 61 | 471 | 294 | 0.0 | 4.2 | 16.8 |

## Faithfulness + monitor catch on cue-following flips (per arm)

cue_ack = CoT references the cue (FAITHFUL/monitorable). against should LOWER it, toward RAISE it. mon_naive = naive monitor (CoT only). mon_terse = adversary-aware terse flag. adv_catch = mon_naive OR mon_terse.

| arm | n_flip | cue_ack% [CI] | mon_naive% [CI] | mon_terse% | adv_catch% [CI] | aw mean | aw med |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| base | 103 | 100 [100,100] | 99 [97,100] | 7 | 99 [97,100] | 551 | 399 |
| faith_against@0.75 | 127 | 68 [60,76] | 68 [60,76] | 3 | 68 [60,76] | 364 | 325 |
| faith_against@1.0 | 210 | 27 [21,33] | 35 [29,42] | 2 | 36 [30,42] | 314 | 289 |
| faith_toward@0.75 | 132 | 99 [98,100] | 98 [96,100] | 6 | 98 [96,100] | 471 | 372 |
| faith_ctrl@0.75 | 60 | 77 [65,87] | 73 [62,83] | 10 | 75 [63,85] | 336 | 300 |

| faith_toward_m@0.75 | 232 | 96 [93,98] | 95 [92,97] | 0 | 95 [92,97] | 292 | 271 |

## Coherence-filtered concealment (exclude degenerate/truncated flips)

A 'concealment' that is really garbling/looping does NOT count. Among COHERENT flips (not degenerate, not truncated), the cue_ack + adv_catch, and the coherent concealed tail (cue_ack=False & coherent) that evades the adversary-aware monitor.

| arm | n_flip | n_coherent_flip | cue_ack%(coherent) | adv_catch%(coherent) | coherent-concealed (cue_ack=F,¬degen,¬trunc) | of those: adv-evading | aw med |

## Selective concealment vs generic shortening — summary

length_ret = aw_median(arm)/aw_median(base); ack_ret = cue_ack(arm)/cue_ack(base). SELECTIVE if ack drops MORE than length (ack_ret << length_ret) AND mon_terse stays low; GENERIC if concealment tracks shortening / mon_terse catches it.

| arm | length_ret | ack_ret | cue_ack(base->arm) | adv_catch(base->arm) | mon_terse(base->arm) |

| rand:10:149:4 | 0.64 | 1.00 | 100->100 | 99->99 | 7->24 |

The genuinely independent quantity (cue_ack and mon_naive are collinear on an explicit cue): among CONCEALED flips, what do the monitors still catch?

| rand:10:149:4 | 0 | - | - | - | - |

## Adversary-aware (terse) flag ALARM VOLUME (precision cost)

If mon_terse fires on most outputs, a 'catch' is a terseness alarm, not concealment detection. Reported: mon_terse on each arm's flips / correct answers / non-flips.

| arm | mon_terse% flips | mon_terse% correct | mon_terse% non-flips |
|---|--:|--:|--:|

| base | 7 | 0 | 0 |

| rand:10:149:4 | 24 | 0 | 1 |

## Matched-length selectivity (faith_against@0.75 vs base cue_ack by word-bin)

If the AGAINST arm conceals MORE than base AT EQUAL LENGTH, the concealment is selective (a mechanism beyond shortening). If cue_ack is only lower in short bins, it is generic shortening.

KEY: base cue_ack is 100% at EVERY length bin, so length has ZERO effect on base acknowledgment -> the AGAINST arm concealing at ANY length (esp. the longest bins) is necessarily a CONTENT effect, not length-mediated shortening.

| aw bin | base cue_ack% (n) | faith_against@0.75 cue_ack% (n) |

| 0-60 | 100 (3) | 100 (1) |
| 60-150 | 100 (6) | 90 (10) |

| 300-∞ | 100 (70) | 67 (73) |

content.text:
===WORKER 7 (gradient steering) assistant text — search for gL10/bullet/formatting===
The gradient-training path works (autograd through the dequantized MoE, loss decreases, sane vector norm). Even this tiny 40-step run already induces terse strongly but not bullet — exactly the structural question to test properly. Let me check the full TRAIN data scale and sequence lengths.
**Confirmed — this is the key finding.** The single fixed gradient-trained vector (gL8) produces ALL-CAPS reasoning *only* when all_caps is requested (upper=1.00) and normal case otherwise (none=0.04). The fixed additive vector is **effectively instruction-conditional** — it learned to *modulate* the in-context-instruction circuit, which the diff-of-means (all_caps 0→0%) could NOT do. This is the tier-1 hypothesis. The decisive test now: does it generalize to held-out **formatting (bullet)**? Let me probe held-out raw-compliance.
🎯 **Decisive result:** the single fixed gradient-trained vector gL8 (2880 params, one layer) achieves **bullet 0→51%** held-out — reproducing FT's signature formatting win (FT: 0→52%) that the diff-of-means single-layer steering got **0%**. Plus terse 10→69%, numbered/xml partial. malformed 0%, genuine bullets. This is the tier-1 outcome. Let me train the alternative candidates + control twin for a proper VAL-based selection, then run the rigorous held-out eval.
- **VAL effective_control macro Δ:** gL10 **+20.0**, gL12 +19.4, gML +16.9, gL8 +14.4, gL6 +13.8 — all accuracy-preserving (VAL acc 94-96% vs base 96.8%), malformed 0%.
VAL selects **gL10** (single-layer, +20.0, acc-preserving). Let me train the matched control `gL10ctrl`, then run the rigorous held-out eval with base/main/control/FT + multi-layer + gL8.
gL10 arm done. Let me preview gL10's held-out raw-compliance while the remaining arms generate.
gL10 (VAL-selected) recovers **bullet 0→33%, terse 10→69%** held-out (gL8 had bullet 51%). Both clearly recover formatting — the tier-1 result is layer-robust. VAL (no formatting) selected gL10; gL8 is a stronger-formatting sensitivity arm. Let me wait for the remaining arms, then judge.
Strong rigorous result, but I see a **contradiction**: bullet shows effective_control 30.8% but "main raw 0.0%" — effective can't exceed raw. Let me check the `raw_compliant`/`effective` functions.
I misread — there's no bug. Recounting: gL10 bullet effective **30.8%** (raw 33.3%), gL8 bullet **48.7%** (= FT 48.7% exactly), gML 35.9%. The headline is rigorous and excellent:
**gL10 (VAL-selected, 2880 params): all-held-out effective_control +11.4pp [+8.3,+14.8] ≈ FT's +11.7; cross-category formatting +11.5 [+6.4,+17.3] (vs Seg-6 diff-of-means +0.0); NO accuracy cost (82.5% vs base 81.1%, vs Seg-6's −12pp); control twin 0.0% (clean dissociation); malformed 0%.** Let me verify no language-shift artifact and build the judge re-validation/inspection.
No language-shift artifact (0/44 gL10, 0/53 gL8, 0/45 gML effective rows). Now the judge re-validation + the (now-positive) "prompt-specific form?" inspection:
Genuine bullets when asked, correct answers. Let me verify the conditionality — that the form is ABSENT on the no-instruction condition:
**Conditionality confirmed:** under gL10 the no-instruction (`none`) traces are normal prose (no bullets/caps), while instruction-present traces produce the asked form. A single FIXED 2880-param vector is effectively instruction-conditional — it modulates the instruction-following circuit. This is the decisive tier-1 result. Let me commit and update the log.
- **Multi-layer DOM `pooled@8,12,16,c0.1`: VAL eff +38.8** (vs Seg-6 single-layer +22, gL10 +20) — strongest within-category, but ~12pp accuracy cost (none-acc 85% vs base 97%).
- **FT-vs-base `ftbase@8,c0.25`: VAL eff +11.9, NO accuracy cost** (none-acc 96.7%).
The headline plot clearly shows the story: gradient-trained recovers formatting (bullet) where diff-of-means is 0, while diff-of-means uniquely gets no_word_so. Let me check the DOM runs' progress.
The diff-of-means runs are slow (random-null arms ramble to the cap). While they run, let me verify the gradient-trained formatting compliance is genuine (not shallow/vacuous) across formatting types.
Formatting compliance is genuine + accurate (numbered 3/3 correct, xml 5/6, terse 20/26, all genuine reasoning). Let me commit and wait for the DOM runs.
Multi-layer diff-of-means (P2): **within-category +26.2pp [+22.1,+30.3]** (strongest of any method — no_word_so 0→69%, terse 0→72%) but **cross-category formatting +0.0** (structural, beats null, sign-specific) and a ~10pp accuracy cost. Confirms multi-layer strengthens within-category but does NOT do formatting. Now P3/P4:
- **NO fixed diff-of-means direction recovers formatting** (cross-category always +0.0): per-category (cat_length +12.8 within, cat_casing can't even do held-out initial_caps), FT-vs-base (weak +1.1, no formatting), and the single-layer pooled reference **replicates Seg-6 exactly (+7.7 all / +13.8 within)**.
- **Only the gradient-trained vector does formatting** → the gradient objective is the cause.
Now the diff-of-means comparison plot (showing cross-category=0 for all fixed directions, only gradient-trained recovers it):
The central-ablation plot is clear: cross-category formatting = 0 for every fixed diff-of-means direction, only gradient-trained recovers it. Let me update the progress log and continuation context, then commit.
Excellent, thorough review. Let me address the actionable items. First the quick data checks (no Modal): (B) quantitative spurious-form rate on the full none set, (C) edited-surface check for incidental formatting leaking into TRAIN targets.
- **(B) gL10 none-condition: 0/39 bullets, 0/39 numbered, 0/39 caps** — the asked form NEVER appears spuriously (hard conditionality evidence). But degenerate 15% vs base 5%, aw 164 vs 129 — confirming the reviewer's point that it mildly increases verbosity (will scope the claim).
- **(C) TRAIN complying targets: 0% bullet-structured, 0.3% any "- " line** — formatting does NOT leak into TRAIN; bullet transfer is genuine cross-category generalization, not memorization.
Now the multi-seed robustness re-train (reviewer #7) + a norm-matched random control (reviewer #10b). Let me train gL10 at seeds 1, 2 and build a random vector control:
Now the limitations and Segment-8 recommendation (pre-commit to gL10):
gL10 seed 1: bullet raw 41% (seed 0 was 33%) — formatting recovery is seed-stable. Let me wait for seed 2 + random control.

- **Seed-stable:** bullet 30.8/35.9/38.5% (seeds 0/1/2), cross-cat 11.5/13.5/13.5%, all-held-out 12.5/13.1/13.1%, accuracy 81.7-82.5% — all stable.
- **Random matched-norm control (‖v‖=148 at L10): 0% bullet, 0.3% all** — rules out "just a big push," confirms it's the LEARNED direction.
The three models agree: the **behavioral** claim is sound (a fixed prompt-independent vector → prompt-dependent form ⇒ conditionality comes from prompt interaction), but the **mechanistic** "modulates the instruction-following circuit" is over-specific — safer framings are "amplifies/unlocks pre-existing instruction-following" or "suppresses the default CoT-style prior." Let me soften the mechanistic language and add the memorized-conjunction rebuttal (TRAIN targets had 0% bullets).

Two tidy items: (1) soften "including formatting"/"matches FT" to reflect partial formatting recovery; (2) fix the conditionality citation — the "0/39 / upper=1.00" numerics need to be traceable (all_caps is a VAL/train-check instruction, not held-out; the 0/39 none counts were computed ad hoc). One last consistency fix — the central-ablation note still says "including formatting":
**The central question is answered at TIER 1 (strongest monitorability threat):** a single **fixed gradient-trained 2,880-parameter additive vector** (one residual-stream layer, **model weights frozen**, complying-target completion-only NLL — the same objective family as the LoRA FT) reproduces fine-tuning's held-out CoT-controllability profile, **recovering the instruction-conditional formatting (bullet) transfer that Segment-6's diff-of-means could not**:

- **Central ablation (P2-P4):** every fixed diff-of-means direction (multi-layer pooled, per-category, FT-vs-base) gets cross-category formatting **+0.0** → the **gradient objective**, not multi-layer/provenance/per-category, is the cause. Multi-layer DOM is the strongest within-category booster (+26pp) but with an accuracy cost + language-shift artifact + zero formatting.

content.text:
Direction structure makes sense: style/casing/length align with the pooled direction (cos ~0.5–0.7); suppression is nearly orthogonal (the "remove a token" operation is fundamentally different); formatting isn't a TRAIN category, so bullet-transfer is the key discriminating test. Now let me run the cheap probe with magnitudes informed by the ~0.03 natural ratio:
The c=1.0 arm garbles into long non-terminating generations (useful: that's past the garbling threshold). Let me let it finish and set up the writeup files meanwhile:
Excellent — clear regime: c=1.0 → 100% malformed (garbled), c=0.5 starts garbling, **c=0.25 at layer 12** looks like the sweet spot (no_the 0→88%, length→100%, low malformed). Let me inspect actual steered traces for coherence:
- **c=1.0 → 100% malformed (garbled); c=0.5 starts garbling (empty/looping); c=0.25 = coherent sweet spot** (no_the 0→88%, length→100%, low malformed).
- The pooled direction at c=0.25 induces a **language/style shift (German/Spanish) + terse** form — it "complies" with no_the/length partly by switching language. This is the edited-surface/style confound made concrete (the direction is style/length-dominated, ~0 formatting component).
Stage A done ($2.24). The sweet spot is **c=0.25 at mid-late layers** (low malformed/degenerate, raises 3/4 VAL instructions); c=0.35 garbles at layers 12/14. Now Stage B: run the top candidates at fuller mnt with both signs for a clean effective_control selection. Let me launch it:
The steered held-out traces are **coherent (not garbled)** — good, no re-selection needed. But on these ARC tasks L8_c0.25's effect looks mild (no formatting forms induced; terse only partially). Let me peek at more sources/instructions where the effect should be stronger (gsm8k/obqa terse, no_word_so, child_explanation):
Clear picture: L8_c0.25 on held-out **partially compresses (terse) and shifts style/language** (raises terse_25w/no_word_so/child_explanation) but does **NOT induce formatting/casing** (bullet/initial_caps stay 0%), and a **Spanish/Spanglish language-shift artifact** appears in some traces — the edited-surface confound. This is shaping up as an honest "partial, no formatting transfer" result. Let me wait for the random arms to finish:
- **Carried by no_word_so (0→38.5%) + terse_25w (10→41%); cross_category formatting = +0.0%** — steering does NOT reproduce FT's bullet/numbered win.
The discriminating evidence is decisive: **REAL does NOT produce the prompt-specific form** (no caps for initial_caps, no bullets for bullet) — it produces a fixed terse/style/suppression shift, helping only where that blend ≈ the asked form (terse_25w, no_word_so). This confirms the direction is a fixed blend, not instruction-conditional form control. Let me run a robustness check (does a stronger magnitude induce formatting, or is it structural?) and generate plots:
The per-instruction plot tells the story crisply (steering wins terse + no_word_so; FT wins terse + bullet + numbered — different carriers). Let me view the macro/null plot:
Both held-out plots are clear and communicate the findings well. Let me check the formatting-robustness result:
The robustness check is still generating (higher magnitudes garble → slow). Let me fill in the write-up held-out section while it runs:
**Robustness confirmed: even at a stronger magnitude (L14_c0.25), formatting stays 0%** (bullet/numbered/section/initial_caps all 0%; pushing harder garbles terse at 33% malformed rather than inducing new forms). So the 0% formatting transfer is structural, not a magnitude issue. Now let me run the within-distribution TRAIN sanity check for the selected config (required by the rubric):
Within-distribution TRAIN check confirms the direction **works in-distribution** (no_the 17→46%, brief_50w 50→88%) but all_caps stays 0→0% — same category pattern as held-out (length/suppression move, casing/formatting don't). This disambiguates "doesn't work" from "doesn't generalize." Let me add it to the write-up:
The **Spanish ablation decisively resolves #6**: removing the Spanish (or whole style) component does NOT reduce suppression/length compliance — it slightly *increases* it (no_answer_word 60→67→73%, under_70w 93→100%). So the win is genuine terse/length compression, not a language-shift side effect. Let me check the shallow-layer sweep selection:
The write-up is comprehensive. Two cleanups: mark it COMPLETE and reconcile the probe bullet (which overstated "complies by switching language" — later corrected by the ablation):
The final review confirms must-haves satisfied and fixes resolved, but flags a real internal tension: my Segment-7 #1 (FT-vs-base diff) over-claims for formatting (it's also TRAIN-derived, so by my own structural logic it lacks a bullet component), and claim (ii) overstates non-conditionality. Let me reconcile these and fix the apples-to-oranges FT comparison:
Now let me reconcile the Segment-7 recommendation priority (the HIGH issue — FT-vs-base diff is also TRAIN-derived, so it can't be the clear formatting lever):
- ✅ **Controlled held-out eval** (9 instr incl. bullet × 39 tasks vs base + 5-seed random null + sign-reversed + FT benchmark; per-instruction McNemar + cluster-bootstrap macro CIs; explicit null/sign judgment; JSONL saved)
- ✅ **Investigations/ablations**: Spanish-removal ablation, shallow-layer sweep, formatting-at-higher-magnitude robustness, language-shift quantification, accuracy-scorer check
- ✅ **Scope respected** (single-layer only; no multi-layer/trained/sparse/Seg-8 built)
**Headline result:** Single-layer steering gives a genuine, sign-specific, null-beating held-out `effective_control` uplift of **+7.7pp [+4.8,+10.5]** (within-category +13.8) — but it's **PARTIAL and does NOT reproduce FT**: carried by terse_25w + no_word_so, **zero formatting transfer** (FT's bullet 0→49% win), a ~12pp accuracy cost, acting as a fixed terse/suppression CoT-style shift rather than instruction-conditional form control. Single-layer is insufficient → Segment 7 should escalate.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate clean, single-panel figures for the write-up. PNG + PDF into ./final_plots/."""
import os
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

OUT = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "final_plots")
os.makedirs(OUT, exist_ok=True)

plt.rcParams.update({
    "figure.dpi": 140,
    "font.size": 12,
    "axes.titlesize": 13,
    "axes.titleweight": "bold",
    "axes.spines.top": False,
    "axes.spines.right": False,
    "axes.grid": True,
    "grid.alpha": 0.25,
    "grid.linewidth": 0.6,
    "legend.frameon": False,
})

C_BASE = "#9aa0a6"
C_FT = "#1f77b4"
C_STEER = "#d1495b"
C_CTRL = "#cfcfcf"


def save(fig, name):
    for ext in ("png", "pdf"):
        fig.savefig(os.path.join(OUT, f"{name}.{ext}"), bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close(fig)


# ---------- Fig 1: headline — held-out CoT-control uplift over base ----------
def fig_headline():
    labels = ["Fine-tuning\n(LoRA)", "Steering\nvector", "Trained-on-\ncontrol vector",
              "Random\nvector", "Sign-\nreversed"]
    uplift = [12.3, 12.8, 0.0, -0.1, 0.0]
    # CIs (pp) for the two real methods; controls have no meaningful uplift CI
    lo = [10.4, 10.7, None, None, None]
    hi = [14.2, 14.9, None, None, None]
    colors = [C_FT, C_STEER, C_CTRL, C_CTRL, C_CTRL]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6.4, 4.0))
    x = np.arange(len(labels))
    for i in range(len(labels)):
        err = None
        if lo[i] is not None:
            err = [[uplift[i] - lo[i]], [hi[i] - uplift[i]]]
        ax.bar(x[i], uplift[i], color=colors[i], width=0.66,
               yerr=err, capsize=5, ecolor="#333", error_kw={"lw": 1.4})
    ax.axhline(10, ls="--", lw=1.2, color="#444")
    ax.text(4.35, 10.4, "minimum\ninteresting\neffect (+10)", fontsize=9, ha="right", va="bottom", color="#444")
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(labels, fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylabel("Held-out CoT-control uplift\nover base (percentage points)")
    ax.set_title("A frozen-weights steering vector matches fine-tuning")
    ax.set_ylim(-2, 17)
    for i in (0, 1):
        ax.text(x[i], hi[i] + 0.4, f"+{uplift[i]:.1f}", ha="center", fontsize=10, fontweight="bold")
    save(fig, "fig1_headline")


# ---------- Fig 2: per-instruction profile ----------
def fig_per_instruction():
    instr = ["bullet list", "terse (\u226425 words)", "numbered list", "omit 'so'",
             "title case", "use phrase twice", "section headers", "XML tags", "explain to a child"]
    base = [0, 14, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
    ft = [52, 61, 9, 3, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
    steer = [48, 69, 8, 0, 1, 1, 0, 2, 0]
    x = np.arange(len(instr)); w = 0.27
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8.2, 4.2))
    ax.bar(x - w, base, w, label="Base", color=C_BASE)
    ax.bar(x, ft, w, label="Fine-tuning", color=C_FT)
    ax.bar(x + w, steer, w, label="Steering vector", color=C_STEER)
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(instr, rotation=35, ha="right", fontsize=9.5)
    ax.set_ylabel("Held-out CoT-control rate (%)")
    ax.set_title("Both gains concentrate in the same few instructions")
    ax.legend(loc="upper right")
    ax.set_ylim(0, 78)
    save(fig, "fig2_per_instruction")


# ---------- Fig 3: gradient objective unlocks formatting ----------
def fig_gradient_vs_dom():
    methods = ["Diff-of-means\n(single layer)", "Diff-of-means\n(multi-layer)",
               "Gradient-trained\nvector", "Fine-tuning"]
    within = [13.8, 26.2, 13.3, 10.0]
    cross = [0.0, 0.0, 11.5, 15.2]
    x = np.arange(len(methods)); w = 0.36
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.2, 4.1))
    ax.bar(x - w / 2, within, w, label="Trained instruction types", color="#8e9bb3")
    ax.bar(x + w / 2, cross, w, label="Held-out formatting (never trained)", color=C_STEER)
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(methods, fontsize=9.5)
    ax.set_ylabel("Held-out CoT-control uplift (pp)")
    ax.set_title("Only the gradient objective transfers to formatting")
    ax.legend(loc="upper left")
    ax.set_ylim(0, 29)
    save(fig, "fig3_gradient_vs_dom")


# ---------- Fig 4: Chen et al. replication ----------
def fig_cot_vs_output():
    instr = ["all caps", "all lowercase", "no commas", "no 'the'"]
    cot = [0.0, 0.0, 14.8, 12.2]
    out = [60.2, 78.6, 79.1, 88.8]
    x = np.arange(len(instr)); w = 0.36
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6.6, 4.0))
    ax.bar(x - w / 2, cot, w, label="Reasoning trace (CoT)", color=C_STEER)
    ax.bar(x + w / 2, out, w, label="Final answer (output)", color=C_FT)
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(instr, fontsize=10.5)
    ax.set_ylabel("Instruction-compliance rate (%)")
    ax.set_title("The model controls its answer, not its reasoning")
    ax.legend(loc="upper left")
    ax.set_ylim(0, 100)
    save(fig, "fig4_cot_vs_output")


# ---------- Fig 5: mechanism, attention vs value ----------
def fig_mechanism():
    conds = ["bullet list", "numbered list"]
    pattern = [71, 62]
    value = [20, 16]
    x = np.arange(len(conds)); w = 0.36
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5.8, 4.0))
    ax.bar(x - w / 2, pattern, w, label="Attention pattern (where it looks)", color="#3a7d44")
    ax.bar(x + w / 2, value, w, label="Carried value (what it adds)", color="#b0b0b0")
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(conds, fontsize=10.5)
    ax.set_ylabel("Share of induced effect reproduced (%)")
    ax.set_title("The vector works by redirecting attention")
    ax.legend(loc="upper right", fontsize=9.5)
    ax.set_ylim(0, 85)
    save(fig, "fig5_mechanism")


# ---------- Fig 6: monitorability ----------
def fig_monitorability():
    scen = ["Verifiable setting\n(answer is wrong)", "Hard-to-verify setting\n(answer is correct)"]
    cot_base = [99, 78]
    cot_steer = [68, 39]
    verifier = [93, 16]
    x = np.arange(len(scen)); w = 0.26
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.0, 4.2))
    ax.bar(x - w, cot_base, w, label="CoT-reading monitor, base", color=C_BASE)
    ax.bar(x, cot_steer, w, label="CoT-reading monitor, steered", color=C_STEER)
    ax.bar(x + w, verifier, w, label="Answer-checking monitor, steered", color=C_FT)
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(scen, fontsize=10)
    ax.set_ylabel("Monitor catch rate (%)")
    ax.set_title("Concealment fools CoT readers; a verifier only helps\nwhen the answer is wrong")
    ax.legend(loc="upper right", fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylim(0, 108)
    save(fig, "fig6_monitorability")


# ---------- Appendix A: dose-response ----------
def fig_dose():
    scale = [0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0, 1.25, 1.5, 2.0]
    bullet = [0, 0, 0, 6, 56, 46, 43, 23]
    terse = [10, 17, 27, 44, 67, 71, 73, 48]
    none_degen = [15, 8, 10, 15, 21, 31, 29, 54]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6.4, 4.0))
    ax.plot(scale, bullet, "-o", color=C_STEER, label="bullet-list control (when asked)")
    ax.plot(scale, terse, "-s", color=C_FT, label="terse control (when asked)")
    ax.plot(scale, none_degen, "--^", color=C_BASE, label="degeneration with no instruction")
    ax.axvline(1.0, ls=":", color="#444", lw=1.2)
    ax.text(1.02, 5, "selected\nstrength", fontsize=9, color="#444")
    ax.set_xlabel("Steering strength (multiple of trained vector norm)")
    ax.set_ylabel("Rate (%)")
    ax.set_title("Dose-response of the steering vector")
    ax.legend(loc="upper left", fontsize=9)
    ax.set_ylim(0, 80)
    save(fig, "figA_dose_response")


# ---------- Appendix B: reasoning-effort interaction ----------
def fig_effort():
    effort = ["low", "medium", "high"]
    base = [19.4, 6.2, 0.0]
    steer = [46.5, 37.5, 29.9]
    x = np.arange(len(effort)); w = 0.36
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5.8, 4.0))
    ax.bar(x - w / 2, base, w, label="Base", color=C_BASE)
    ax.bar(x + w / 2, steer, w, label="Steering vector", color=C_STEER)
    ax.set_xticks(x); ax.set_xticklabels(effort, fontsize=11)
    ax.set_xlabel("Reasoning effort")
    ax.set_ylabel("CoT-control rate on carrier\ninstructions (%)")
    ax.set_title("The uplift survives where base control collapses")
    ax.legend(loc="upper right")
    ax.set_ylim(0, 55)
    save(fig, "figB_effort")


for f in (fig_headline, fig_per_instruction, fig_gradient_vs_dom, fig_cot_vs_output,
          fig_mechanism, fig_monitorability, fig_dose, fig_effort):
    f()
print("done:", sorted(os.listdir(OUT)))
